canonical_pages.title: Optimal quest guide
canonical_pages.data_contained: THE canonical recommended quest-completion order for members main accounts, optimized to minimize manual skill training. The ordered table is the data: each row is a quest (or an inline 'train skill X to level Y' directive, or a non-quest activity), in recommended sequence. Columns: Quest/Activity name (wikilinked), Quick Guide link, 'New levels after quest' (cumulative skill levels shown via skill icons, computed from XP rewards), Quest points, cumulative Total QP, Additional info/notes, Location/bring-from-bank. Also has prose 'Notable quest unlocks' sections (skill/area/transport/equipment unlocks). VERIFIED: page exists (pageid 82102), members-only, ordered top-to-bottom starting at Learning the Ropes; {{Sync}} marker present in raw wikitext; links out to /Ironman and /Free-to-play variants.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED via ?action=raw. Row ORDER is the primary signal and is cleanly parseable. Skill rewards encoded inline via {{Optimal quest|skill=xp}} (lowercase skill=integer-XP pairs); training steps via {{Optimal quest/train|skill|level}}; QP via {{Optimal quest/qp|points}}; non-quest activities via {{Optimal quest/action|...}}. NOT backed by a Lua module — cumulative levels are computed at render time via MediaWiki {{#vardefine}} variables (raw wikitext opens with a {{#vardefine:}} block initialising all 24 skills + questpoints to 0), so rendered HTML shows skill icons/tooltips rather than plain numbers (limits HTML scraping). Best to parse raw wikitext (?action=raw) and extract template params. 'Additional info' column is free prose.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Questreq/data
canonical_pages.data_contained: NEW / MOST MACHINE-PARSEABLE: a Lua data module (~1,900 lines) holding, per quest (plus miniquests and achievement diaries), its prerequisite QUESTS array and its skill-requirement array of [skill, level] pairs with optional modifiers ('ironman', 'boostable'). Example: ['Animal Magnetism'] = {['quests']={'Ernest the Chicken','Priest in Peril','The Restless Ghost'}, ['skills']={{'Crafting',19},{'Prayer',31,'ironman'},{'Ranged',30}}}. This is the quest DEPENDENCY GRAPH (prerequisites), not an editorial recommended order — but a topological sort of it yields all VALID completion orders, and it directly supplies the per-quest requirement metadata the wikitable encodes only as rendered icons.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED: clean, consistently-formatted Lua tables; carries an 'ironman' modifier so account-type-aware requirement gating is built in (e.g. Prayer 31 ironman-only). This is the structured backing the proposed map wrongly said 'does not exist'. Fetch as raw Lua (?action=raw) or via api.php action=expandtemplates / the data dumps. Use it to (a) join requirement metadata onto the ordered guide rows and (b) derive/validate a quest order via topological sort when the editorial wikitext order is unavailable. Does NOT contain the editorial 'minimise training' ordering — that lives only in the guide wikitext.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Questreq
canonical_pages.data_contained: Companion Lua module that consumes Module:Questreq/data to render quest- and skill-requirement tables (recursive prerequisite expansion; categorises skill reqs; flags Ironman-only and (boostable) requirements). Documents the semantics of the /data fields (including the 'ironman' and 'boostable' modifiers).
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED: presentation/logic layer over /data. Does NOT do topological sorting, cycle detection, or produce an ordered sequence — it assumes acyclic data and only displays requirements. Useful for confirming how to interpret /data (especially ironman flags); the actual structured payload is Module:Questreq/data.

canonical_pages.title: Optimal quest guide/Ironman
canonical_pages.data_contained: Canonical recommended quest order for standard Ironman accounts. Same table format/columns as the main guide with ironman-specific routing. VERIFIED: opens 'This guide is intended for iron accounts'; ordered sequence from Learning the Ropes ('Following the guide fairly closely until the completion of Holy Grail is recommended'). Contains only passing tactical notes for hardcore ironmen (e.g. do certain quests first for safety) — it is NOT a dedicated HCIM/GIM order page.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED via WebFetch. Same encoding as the main guide: inline {{Optimal quest|...}} / {{Optimal quest/train|...}} / {{Optimal quest/qp|...}} over a wikitable; {{#vardefine}}-based cumulative levels; not module-backed. Parse raw wikitext. This page is the de-facto source for standard Ironman and (by editorial intent) HCIM; there are NO separate canonical HCIM/UIM/GIM optimal-ORDER pages in the main namespace.

canonical_pages.title: Optimal quest guide/Free-to-play
canonical_pages.data_contained: Recommended quest order for free-to-play main accounts (~23 quests, grouped by region rather than strict skill gating, ending at Dragon Slayer I). Same column structure plus a Grand Exchange item-cost table (via {{CostTableHead}}) and a 'Bring from bank' column (items via {{plink}}).
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED via ?action=raw: DOES use the {{Optimal quest|skill=xp}} / {{Optimal quest/qp|n}} template family (an earlier rendered-HTML read mistakenly reported plain rows — corrected). {{Sync}} marker and {{#vardefine:colspan|7}} block present. F2P sub-domain of the same canonical guide family. The path-style title is canonical; 'Optimal free-to-play quest guide' 404s (not a redirect).

canonical_pages.title: Optimal quest guide/Free-to-play/Ironman
canonical_pages.data_contained: Recommended quest order for free-to-play Ironman accounts. VERIFIED via ?action=raw: ~25 quest/activity rows, final cumulative 49 QP (proposed map's '~17 quests / 45 QP' was wrong — corrected). Opens 'Unlike the standard optimal quest guide, this optimal free-to-play Ironman quest guide focuses mostly on optimizing the progress through many different quests' (parallel completion over XP rewards).
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED: uses {{Optimal quest|...}} templates (e.g. {{Optimal quest|hitpoints=1154}}). Carries an {{Incomplete|copied from the F2P optimal quest guide page, need to provide alternate strategies and optimized order without the use of GE}} banner — so treat its order as provisional/under construction. Covers the F2P-ironman corner of the variant matrix.

canonical_pages.title: Template:Optimal quest (and /qp, /train, /action helpers)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Helper template family that encodes per-quest skill XP rewards inside every Optimal quest guide table. {{Optimal quest|skill=xp|...}} takes lowercase skill=integer-XP pairs for 24 skills (attack, defence, strength, hitpoints, ranged, prayer, magic, cooking, woodcutting, fletching, fishing, firemaking, crafting, smithing, mining, herblore, agility, thieving, slayer, farming, runecraft, hunter, construction, sailing). Sibling templates: {{Optimal quest/qp}} (quest points), {{Optimal quest/train|skill|level}} (manual training milestones), {{Optimal quest/action}} (non-quest activities).
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED: documentation lists 24 skills INCLUDING Sailing (proposed map said '21 skills' but then listed 24 — corrected to 24). This is the data-encoding layer, not a standalone dataset. Uses MediaWiki {{#vardefine}} parser variables (NOT Lua/#invoke); for any skill the variable skill+'xp' must be predefined upstream. To extract structured XP-per-quest, parse these template invocations out of the guide pages' raw wikitext rather than scraping rendered HTML.

canonical_pages.title: Template:Optimal quest guide/Recommended Quests
canonical_pages.data_contained: Shared transcluded prose block of 'notable quest unlocks' categorized into skill unlocks, area unlocks, transportation unlocks, and equipment unlocks (e.g. Druidic Ritual -> Herblore, Priest in Peril -> Morytania). Provides unlock-rationale context, not the ordered sequence.
canonical_pages.notes: Static hierarchical wiki-markup lists, no Lua module, no structured metadata. Useful as a quest->unlock mapping if you parse the wikilinks, but prose-shaped and unordered. Secondary to the ordered tables and to Module:Questreq/data.

canonical_pages.title: Ultimate Ironman Guide/Quests
canonical_pages.data_contained: UIM quest reference. VERIFIED: NOT a recommended order. Mixed prose + tables organised by UIM obstacle type — 'Notable item requirements' (wikitable), item/equipment limitations (Entrana, Glarial's Tomb), weight requirements, and 'Quests requiring inventory space' (wikitable). Assumes the player already knows which quests to do.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED ruled OUT as an order source for this domain — no sequence, problem-focused not progression-focused, does not use {{Optimal quest}} templates. Included to fill the UIM variant gap: it is the closest UIM-specific quest resource, but UIM optimal ORDER must be derived (Module:Questreq/data topo-sort + UIM constraints), not read off a page.

canonical_pages.title: Quests/List (NOT a quest-order source — relevance check)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Comprehensive directory of ALL quests with difficulty, length, QP, series, release date. Sortable wikitable. Contains NO recommended completion order/path.
canonical_pages.notes: Explicitly ruled OUT for this domain — a quest catalog, not a progression sequence. Cleanly parseable; answers 'what quests exist + requirements', not 'what order'. Good companion table for joining requirement metadata onto ordered guide rows (alongside Module:Questreq/data).

best_machine_readable: Two complementary entry points. (1) For the EDITORIAL recommended order (the 'minimise training' sequence): the raw wikitext of the four main-namespace guide pages via action=raw — https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/w/Optimal_quest_guide?action=raw (and /Ironman, /Free-to-play, /Free-to-play/Ironman). Order = literal sequence of table rows; skill-XP / training / QP encoded as parseable inline templates ({{Optimal quest|skill=xp}}, {{Optimal quest/train|skill|level}}, {{Optimal quest/qp|n}}). Parse raw wikitext, not rendered HTML, because 'New levels' is computed at render-time via {{#vardefine}} and shown as icons. Or use api.php action=parse&prop=wikitext (or action=query&prop=revisions&rvslots=main&rvprop=content) against the same titles. (2) For the STRUCTURED dependency graph + requirements (the genuinely clean machine-readable source the original map missed): Module:Questreq/data (raw Lua, ~1,900 entries: per-quest prerequisite quests + [skill,level] reqs with 'ironman'/'boostable' modifiers). Topologically sort it for any VALID order and to power account-type-aware gating; join it (and Quests/List) onto the editorial rows for difficulty/requirement enrichment. Use the editorial wikitext as the opinionated default order, Module:Questreq/data as the structured validation/fallback layer.
gaps: The editorial OPTIMAL ('minimise training') order itself has no Lua/JSON/CSV backing — it is hand-curated wikitext you must parse. (NOTE: the dependency graph IS structured — Module:Questreq/data — so a valid topological order is machine-derivable; only the editorial optimisation is wikitext-only.)

Recommended skill LEVELS at each guide step are computed cumulatively via {{#vardefine}} and rendered as icons; raw guide wikitext stores only per-quest XP deltas. To get 'level X needed before quest Y' you must either replay XP accumulation (XP->level conversion) from the guide, or read flat requirement levels from Module:Questreq/data.
The 'Additional info'/'Additional steps' column is free-form prose (bring-lists, tactics, parallelisation) and is not reliably parseable into fields.

Variant coverage is incomplete: only main (members), standard Ironman, F2P main, and F2P Ironman have dedicated optimal-ORDER pages. NO dedicated HCIM, UIM, or GIM optimal quest-order page exists in the main namespace — standard Ironman is editorially meant to cover HCIM/GIM; UIM has only Ultimate Ironman Guide/Quests (prose/obstacle-focused, no order, VERIFIED). For these variants, derive order from Module:Questreq/data + the standard Ironman guide.
The F2P Ironman guide carries an {{Incomplete}} banner (copied from F2P, optimised GE-free order not yet done) — treat its specific ordering as provisional.

User-namespace drafts exist (User:Lezed1/Optimal quest guide[/Ironman], User:Dirtybert/OSRS-Group-Ironman-Guide) — non-canonical WIP copies; the Dirtybert page is the only GIM-specific resource but is user-space and must NOT be source of truth.

Ordering is editorial/opinionated and changes with game updates; pages carry a {{Sync}} marker and disclaim being strictly the most XP-efficient order — so periodic re-scrape + revision check is required.
verify_changes: Corrected the map: added Module:Questreq/data (clean parseable Lua dependency-graph + account-type-aware requirements — the structured source the original wrongly said didn't exist) plus Module:Questreq and the UIM Quests page; fixed Template:Optimal quest skill count to 24 (incl. Sailing), fixed F2P-Ironman to ~25 rows/49 QP, confirmed F2P uses the template family, and verified every URL resolves via action=raw/API (page 82102 last edited 2026-06-15, revid 15233825).

domain: Quest data (per-quest requirements: skills/quests/items; XP rewards; item & access unlocks; quest points)
best_machine_readable: The OSRS Wiki Bucket API 'quest' bucket: https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/api.php?action=bucket&format=json&query=bucket('quest').select(...).run() — officially sanctioned for external use ("External users can query Bucket's API to get this information, without needing to scrape or parse wiki pages") and returns JSON per-quest metadata (official_difficulty, official_length, start_point, requirements, items_required, enemies_to_defeat, ironman_concerns, description, json, page_name). VERIFIED LIVE: a select(...).run() query returns those exact fields. Because the quest bucket OMITS quest points, XP rewards, release date, and members status, and stores requirements/items/enemies as inline wikitext (not normalized columns), the practical pipeline is a JOIN: (1) Bucket 'quest' for core details + (2) Module:Questreq/data (?action=raw) for the clean normalized skill+prerequisite-quest DAG with ironman/boostable flags + (3) Quests/List for quest points / difficulty / length / release / members + (4) Quest experience rewards (+ F2P) for fixed-skill XP + (5) Quest item rewards (item unlocks) and per-quest {{Quest rewards}} for access/feature unlocks. The Bucket and Module sources regenerate automatically from quest-page edits.

canonical_pages.title: Bucket API - quest bucket (action=bucket)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Structured per-quest metadata populated by Module:QuestDetails (setBucket -> bucket('quest').put) from each quest page's {{Quest details}} template. VERIFIED LIVE 2026-06-16: a select(...).limit(3).run() query returns a JSON array whose per-quest fields are exactly: page_name, official_difficulty, official_length, start_point, requirements, items_required, enemies_to_defeat, description (and json + page_name_sub per schema). Confirmed it does NOT return quest points, XP rewards, release date, or members status.
canonical_pages.notes: BEST machine-readable entry point and officially sanctioned for external use (RuneScape:Bucket VERIFIED verbatim: 'External users can query Bucket's API to get this information, without needing to scrape or parse wiki pages'). Endpoint: https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/api.php?action=bucket with a query string e.g. bucket('quest').select(...).where(...).run(). PARTIAL because: (1) requirements/items_required/enemies_to_defeat are stored as wikitext/HTML free text (skill levels + quest links inline, not normalized columns) needing secondary parsing; (2) the quest bucket does NOT include quest points, XP rewards, release date, or members status as indexed fields. Pair with Module:Questreq/data for clean requirements and Quests/List for QP/release/members. Distinct from WikiSync (RuneScape:WikiSync), whose separate API is explicitly NOT for third-party use (VERIFIED: 'Please do not use the WikiSync API in your own projects').

canonical_pages.title: Module:Questreq/data
canonical_pages.data_contained: The cleanest fully-normalized source for quest PREREQUISITES only: per quest, a 'quests' array (prerequisite quest names) and a 'skills' array of {skill, level, optional flags}. VERIFIED verbatim 2026-06-16 — the COMPLETE 'Animal Magnetism' entry is: quests = {'Ernest the Chicken','Priest in Peril','The Restless Ghost'}, skills = {{'Crafting',19},{'Prayer',31,'ironman'},{'Ranged',30},{'Slayer',18},{'Woodcutting',35}}. (Note: the previously-proposed map quoted this entry INCOMPLETELY, omitting Slayer 18 and Woodcutting 35 — corrected here.) Also verified 'Between a Rock...' showing the 'boostable' flag: {'Mining',40,'boostable'},{'Smithing',50,'boostable'}.
canonical_pages.notes: Lua data table; fully machine-parseable (deterministic structure, alphabetical, 'The' ignored in sort). Loaded by Module:Questreq and Module:Required for completing. Per-skill 'ironman' flag marks ironman-only level requirements; 'boostable' flag marks boostable requirements. GAPS: no XP rewards, no item requirements, no quest points, no unlocks. Fetch via ?action=raw for the literal Lua. This is the authoritative source-of-truth for the requirements DAG (skills + quest prerequisites). CAUTION: editors must keep entries hand-synced with quest pages, so verify a sample against {{Quest details}} after game updates.

canonical_pages.title: Quest experience rewards
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED: per-skill sections (one table per skill, ~23 skills) each a sortable wikitable mapping quest -> exact XP reward in that skill, plus members flag, skill requirement, and enemy to defeat columns. Includes a 'Skill choice' section for lamp/book flexible rewards, and a 'Quests with no experience rewards' section (split F2P/Members). F2P fixed-skill rewards are split out to a sibling page.
canonical_pages.notes: Authoritative for fixed-skill XP reward amounts. PARTIAL: hand-maintained tables with XP values hardcoded per row (verified values like 1,000.5 / 2,812.4); requires HTML/wikitext table parsing and values are entered manually (drift risk; not backed by a module/bucket). 'Skill choice' rewards (lamps/books the player allocates) cannot be assigned to a fixed skill and must be modeled separately.

canonical_pages.title: Quest experience rewards (F2P)
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED: 'There are a total of 12 free-to-play quests that give experience rewards.' Same shape as the main XP page but restricted to F2P: 'Set experience' (fixed-skill) and 'Skill choice' (lamp) sections, with quest, XP amount, requirements, enemy columns, plus a per-skill summary/total table at the top.
canonical_pages.notes: Use alongside the members XP page if F2P/members account scoping matters. Same manual-table caveats (hardcoded values, no backing module).

canonical_pages.title: Quests/List
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED 2026-06-16 master table of every quest (now 24 F2P / 156 members = 180 quests; totals 46 F2P QP + 289 members QP = 335 QP — note totals updated since the original audit's '46/289'): quest number/ID, name, difficulty, length, QUEST POINTS awarded, series, release date, F2P-vs-members grouping (sortable wikitables grouped by membership). Also carries league-completability notes.
canonical_pages.notes: Best single source for quest points per quest, official difficulty/length, series, release date, members status, and the canonical quest roster + IDs. Fills the QP/release/series/members gaps left by the quest Bucket. Sortable wikitable, cleanly parseable. Re-check totals after each new quest release (counts/QP move).

canonical_pages.title: Quests/Requirements by quest
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED: quest-indexed requirement tables grouped by difficulty tier (Novice/Intermediate/Experienced/Master/Grandmaster, Special, Miniquests) with skill-icon+level requirements, prerequisite quests, QP prerequisites, combat-level needs, and toughest enemy (level + safespot/avoidable notes); plus a DPL-generated 'Overall Requirements List' table (name, number, difficulty, length, prereqs with boostability, enemy, release date).
canonical_pages.notes: Human-facing aggregate. Mix of hand-maintained wikitables and a DPL (Dynamic Page List) query over pages using the quest templates. For a DB, prefer Module:Questreq/data over scraping this. Asterisks denote boostable/optional. Ironman notes appear as footnotes (verified e.g. Imp Catcher: extra imps optional 'unless ironman Mode').

canonical_pages.title: Quests/Requirements by skill
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED inverse index: per skill, a wikitable of quests requiring that skill with columns Quest / Level / Boostable. Includes difficulty-tier max-requirement summary tables at the top (Novice..Grandmaster). Ironman special cases noted as footnotes.
canonical_pages.notes: Useful as a cross-check/inverse view of Module:Questreq/data. Ironman special cases verified as footnotes (Tai Bwo Wannai Trio: 'Ironmen may need 65 fishing... if they burn the raw karambwan'; The Fremennik Isles: 'Ironmen will need 56 woodcutting'). Same data is cleaner in the module.

canonical_pages.title: Per-quest pages: Template:Quest details + Template:Quest rewards (e.g. Animal Magnetism)
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED fullest single-quest picture. {{Quest details}}: start, startmap, difficulty, length, description, requirements, items (required), recommended, kills/enemies, ironman, league region. {{Quest rewards}}: qp + a 'rewards' blob — verified for Animal Magnetism: XP rewards (Crafting 1,000 / Fletching 1,000 / Slayer 1,000 / Woodcutting 2,500), item unlock (Ava's attractor if Ranged <50 else Ava's accumulator), and access unlock (ability to trade with Ava / purchase her devices).
canonical_pages.notes: This is where ACCESS/FEATURE UNLOCKS live (items granted + area/feature access) — but the {{Quest rewards}} 'rewards' parameter and {{Quest details}} requirements/items are semi-structured free text (links + prose), not normalized fields, so only partially parseable; needs per-quest wikitext parsing. VERIFIED: {{Quest details}} feeds Bucket:Quest via Module:QuestDetails (setBucket); {{Quest rewards}} does NOT populate a queryable bucket. Fetch wikitext via ?action=raw.

canonical_pages.title: Module:QuestDetails (logic) / Bucket:Quest (schema)
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED. Bucket:Quest is the schema page for the queryable 'quest' bucket; fields (all TEXT): description, enemies_to_defeat, ironman_concerns, items_required, official_difficulty, official_length, requirements, start_point, json (+ page_name, page_name_sub). Module:QuestDetails is the logic module (p.details + setBucket -> bucket('quest').put(bucket_data)) that writes this bucket from {{Quest details}}; VERIFIED it handles ONLY details (start/difficulty/length/description/requirements/items/recommended/enemies/ironman/league region) and does NOT process XP rewards or quest points.
canonical_pages.notes: Schema reference for the Bucket API entry point above. Confirms which fields are queryable vs. which (QP, XP, release, members) are NOT in the quest bucket and must come from Quests/List + Quest experience rewards. 'ironman_concerns' is the account-type field.

canonical_pages.title: Quest item rewards
canonical_pages.data_contained: NEW (was missing from the proposed map). Per-quest ITEM rewards (the item unlocks granted on completion), organized into ~6 categorized sections: Weapons, Armour, Miscellaneous, Cosmetics, Coins, and tradeable items obtainable outside quests. Two-column tables: Quest (linked) + Reward (item name/icon/link). Footnotes capture conditional rewards (e.g. Ava's accumulator if Ranged >=50).
canonical_pages.notes: Partially fills the previously-flagged 'unlocks have no normalized source' gap for ITEM rewards specifically. CAVEATS: explicitly tagged incomplete ('This article or section is incomplete'), hand-maintained, NOT sortable, no quantity/members columns, and does NOT cover area/feature/spellbook ACCESS unlocks (those remain only in {{Quest rewards}}). Use as a reward-item index but cross-check against each quest's {{Quest rewards}} blob; not authoritative on its own.

canonical_pages.title: Quest items/Need for quest
canonical_pages.data_contained: NEW (was missing from the proposed map). ITEM-indexed inverse view of quest item REQUIREMENTS: per item, which quest it is found in, which quest(s) it is used in, whether it is genuinely needed, what it is used for, and related achievement-diary tasks. Hand-maintained wikitable.
canonical_pages.notes: Useful inverse CROSS-CHECK for the items_required gap, but NOT a normalized requirements source: item-indexed (not quest-indexed), no quantities, mixes essential items with cosmetics and diary tasks, free-text 'used for' column. For a quest item-requirements DAG, still prefer parsing each quest's {{Quest details}} 'items' field (via Bucket items_required) and use this page only to validate coverage.

gaps: No single page/source contains all of: requirements + XP rewards + item rewards + access unlocks + QP. The data is split across the Bucket API, Module:Questreq/data, Quests/List, Quest experience rewards, and Quest item rewards / per-quest {{Quest rewards}} — a join is mandatory.

ACCESS/FEATURE unlocks (area access, fairy rings, spellbooks, teleports, shop access, etc.) are still NOT in any normalized/queryable store. They live only as free-text in each quest page's {{Quest rewards}} 'rewards' parameter and require per-quest wikitext/NLP parsing. (Quest item rewards covers ITEM rewards only, and is itself incomplete; Optimal quest guide has a curated 'Notable quest unlocks' section but it is selective.)

ITEM rewards now have a partial dedicated source (Quest item rewards) but it is explicitly flagged incomplete, hand-maintained, non-sortable, and lacks quantity/members columns — treat as supplementary, not authoritative.

The quest Bucket does NOT index quest points, XP rewards, release date, or members status — these must be sourced from Quests/List (QP/release/members) and Quest experience rewards (XP). VERIFIED live.
XP reward pages are hand-maintained wikitables with hardcoded values (drift/error risk) and are not backed by a structured module/bucket.

Module:Questreq/data covers only skill + prerequisite-quest requirements; it has no item requirements, no QP-cost prerequisites, no XP, no unlocks. It is also editor-maintained and can lag/omit (the originally-proposed map's own Animal Magnetism quote was incomplete) — sample-validate after updates.

Item REQUIREMENTS (items_required) remain semi-structured free text in {{Quest details}} (item links + quantities + prose); the only item-level index (Quest items/Need for quest) is item-indexed, hand-maintained, and quantity-free, so a normalized per-quest item-requirement list still requires bespoke wikitext parsing.
'Skill choice' XP rewards (lamps/books the player allocates) cannot be mapped to a fixed skill and must be modeled as flexible XP.

Account-type granularity is coarse: the wiki only distinguishes main vs ironman (per-skill 'ironman' flags in Module:Questreq/data and 'ironman_concerns' in Bucket:Quest, plus ironman footnotes on the requirements pages). There are NO distinct HCIM/UIM/GIM quest-requirement variants — those modes inherit ironman requirements.

WikiSync (RuneScape:WikiSync) tracks per-account quest completion but its API is explicitly restricted to wiki internal use (VERIFIED: 'Please do not use the WikiSync API in your own projects'), so it is not a viable third-party data source for completion status.
verify_changes: VERIFIED all 10 originally-listed URLs resolve and match claims (Bucket external-use sanction, WikiSync restriction, Bucket:Quest schema, Module:QuestDetails setBucket-only-details, Quests/List now 180 quests/335 QP). CORRECTED the Module:Questreq/data Animal Magnetism example (it was missing Slayer 18 + Woodcutting 35). ADDED two missing sources: 'Quest item rewards' (partial item-unlock source) and 'Quest items/Need for quest' (item-requirement cross-check). Tightened parseable ratings and gaps accordingly.

domain: Bosses / PVM rates (kill-rate kc/hr by method, entry requirements, notable drops/unlocks)

canonical_pages.title: Bucket API (action=bucket) — money_making_guide bucket (kc/hr + profit + reqs + drops)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Per-method money-making guide records, INCLUDING all 'Killing X' boss guides. CONFIRMED LIVE: this exact query returns {page_name, value:'profit/hr as string', json:'{...}'}. The 'json' blob contains: prices.default_kph (the kc/hr number, e.g. Zulrah=20, Cerberus=50), prices.kph_text ('Kills per hour'), prices.default_value (profit/hr float), members, activity, category, skill (skill reqs text), quest (quest reqs text), other, intensity, version, time, recurrence, and inputs/outputs lists (each item: name/qty/value/isph/pricetype). So nearly ALL useful data (kc/hr, reqs, drops, supplies) lives INSIDE the json blob.
canonical_pages.notes: CORRECTED: The proposed map was WRONG that value/members/skill/quest/category/inputs/outputs are top-level selectable columns. The bucket has ONLY FOUR top-level fields: page_name, value, recurring, json (proven by Module:Mmgtable source: bucket('money_making_guide').put({value, recurring, json=bucket_data}), and by live API errors 'Field members not found' / 'Field skill not found'). You MUST select 'json' and decode it for kc/hr, members, skill, quest, category, intensity, inputs, outputs. The ORIGINAL proposed URL (which selected members/skill/quest/category/inputs/outputs) ERRORS — replaced with a working query. Enumerate all boss guides via an unfiltered bucket('money_making_guide').select('page_name','value','json').limit(N).run() (CONFIRMED returns 'Killing green dragons', etc.). where() supports equality but NOT the '~'/LIKE operator (errors 'Invalid operand') and NOT nested fields like json.default_kph (errors 'Bucket json not found'); filter/match on page_name client-side, or post-filter the json. No account-type variants in this bucket (rates assume a main with buyable supplies).

canonical_pages.title: Bucket:Money making guide (schema page for the bucket)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Authoritative schema + data preview. CONFIRMED top-level schema is only THREE declared fields: value (TEXT), recurring (BOOLEAN), json (TEXT) — plus the implicit page_name. The kc/hr (default_kph + kph_text) and all reqs/inputs/outputs live INSIDE the json TEXT blob, not as columns.
canonical_pages.notes: CORRECTED: confirms the bucket is intentionally narrow (value/recurring/json). best_machine_readable: Bucket API (https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/api.php?action=bucket). Two buckets, joined on boss/page name: (1) money_making_guide for kc/hr + profit + skill/quest reqs + inputs(supplies)/outputs(drops) — but it has ONLY four top-level fields (page_name, value, recurring, json), so you MUST select 'json' and decode the blob for everything except value/recurring. CONFIRMED-LIVE: bucket('money_making_guide').select('page_name','value','json').where('page_name','Money making guide/Killing Zulrah').run() returns json containing prices.default_kph:20 + kph_text:'Kills per hour' (Cerberus=50). CONFIRMED-LIVE: bucket('infobox_monster').select('page_name','combat_level','hitpoints','slayer_level','slayer_experience','elemental_weakness','max_hit').where('page_name','Cerberus').run() returns combat_level:318, hitpoints:600, slayer_level:91, slayer_experience:690, elemental_weakness:'Water'. where() supports equality only (no '~'/LIKE, no nested json.* keys). Schemas: Bucket:Money_making_guide and Bucket:Infobox_monster.
gaps: money_making_guide bucket is NARROW: only page_name/value/recurring/json are top-level. kc/hr (default_kph), kph_text, members, skill, quest, category, intensity, inputs, outputs ALL live inside the 'json' TEXT blob — you must decode it. You cannot filter or select on those nested keys server-side (where('json.default_kph',...) errors 'Bucket json not found'); post-filter client-side after decoding.

where() operator support is limited: equality works, but the '~'/LIKE operator errors ('Invalid operand for bucket.where(): ~'). To get only 'Killing X' boss guides you must enumerate all mmg records (unfiltered .limit().run()) and string-match page_name client-side, or use Category:Money making guides (no dedicated killing/combat subcategory exists).

kc/hr is stored only as a single baseline default_kph per guide. The richer per-gear-setup kc/hr figures (e.g. Cerberus Tbow/Arclight/Bludgeon ~40 vs Scythe ~52) exist only as unstructured PROSE on the per-boss pages — not machine-readable.

No account-type-aware kc/hr anywhere. money_making_guide bucket has no ironman/HCIM/UIM dimension; rates assume a main with buyable supplies. Ironman_money_making_guide is prose + manually-curated tables (NOT bucket-driven) and covers ironman/HCIM/UIM only (no GIM). Account-type viability must be derived (e.g. inputs untradeable/self-gathered).

Not every boss has a 'Killing X' guide, so kc/hr coverage is incomplete (some low-value/quest bosses have no mmg entry). Boss page + infobox_monster bucket cover all bosses for stats/requirements, but kc/hr will be null where no guide exists.

Entry requirements are split: slayer_level + combat stats + weakness in infobox_monster (top-level columns); skill-level + quest reqs as FREE TEXT inside the mmg json blob (skill/quest keys). slayer_level is null for non-slayer bosses (correct). Assembling one normalized 'entry requirements' record needs a cross-bucket join + free-text parsing.

Notable drops/unlocks come through the mmg outputs list (profit-oriented loot, not a complete drop table). Full drop tables live on each monster's own page/infobox (separate domain); collection-log uniques are only semi-structured on the Boss page.
infobox_monster can return multiple rows per boss (e.g. Zulrah returns 3 rows differing by max_hit, per phase/attack) — dedupe on page_name and decide how to aggregate max_hit.

canonical_pages.title: Bucket API — money_making_guide bucket (api.php?action=bucket)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Every money making guide on the wiki as a queryable JSON row (627 rows confirmed live). Each row has top-level fields value (profit, TEXT), recurring (BOOLEAN), and json (TEXT blob). The json decodes to: activity, members (bool), category (e.g. 'Skilling','Combat/Mid','Processing'), skillcategory, skill (skill+level reqs), intensity, quest, isperkill, version, prices (aggregated profit/cost), and crucially inputs[] and outputs[] each as {name, value, qty, pricetype}. This directly encodes the GP/XP-vs-gather split: a gather method (e.g. Motherlode Mine, confirmed: empty inputs, GP-valued ore/nugget outputs, 13.1k-64k Mining XP/hr) has empty inputs; a buy/processing method has GP-valued inputs (mains pay GP) plus outputs.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED LIVE: queried the endpoint; returns a JSON `bucket` array (627 rows). Confirmed sample row: nature-rune crafting with members:true, inputs[]/outputs[] each {name,value,qty} (CORRECTED: fields are `qty` and `pricetype`, NOT quantity/price type). Schema confirmed via Bucket:Money_making_guide (only value/recurring/json are top-level selectable). IMPORTANT CORRECTION: bucket.where() does NOT support LIKE or substring matching on the json blob (tested: LIKE errors 'Invalid operand'; an exact-string where on json returns []). So you cannot server-side filter by method content — fetch all rows and filter client-side. Backed by Module:Mmgtable. Account-type tagging is via category text (e.g. methods tagged 'Ironman'), not a clean per-variant column; no HCIM/UIM/GIM columns. best_machine_readable:
Two complementary, both VERIFIED-live, machine-readable backbones — use them together:

(1) METHODS/MONEY-MAKING cost split — OSRS Wiki Bucket API: https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/api.php?action=bucket&format=json&query=bucket('money_making_guide').select('value','recurring','json').run() . Returns a JSON `bucket` array of all 627 money making guides. Top-level selectable fields are ONLY value (profit), recurring (bool), json (TEXT). JSON-decode `json` client-side to get activity, members, category, skillcategory, skill, intensity, quest, prices, and — most importantly — inputs[] and outputs[] each as {name, value, qty, pricetype} (note: fields are `qty`/`pricetype`). The GP/XP-vs-gather split falls out: gather/free methods have empty inputs[] with GP-valued outputs (e.g. Motherlode Mine — verified, no inputs, ore/nugget outputs, 13.1k-64k Mining XP/hr); buy/processing methods have GP-valued inputs[] (the GP a main spends). CRITICAL: bucket.where() does NOT support LIKE/substring on the json blob — fetch all rows and filter client-side. Schema authored in Module:Mmgtable.

(2) SKILL-TRAINING GP/XP cost split (mains buy inputs) — Module:Skill calc + per-skill data submodules via action=raw, e.g. https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/w/Module:Skill_calc/Smithing?action=raw . Each skill submodule (~20 skills) is a clean uniform Lua table of methods {name, level, xp, materials[]{name,quantity}, outputQuantity/outputItem, members, type, title}; ~170 entries for Smithing. Module:Skill calc prices materials/outputs against Module:GEPrices/data.json to compute GP/XP. This is a STRICTLY better, uniform-schema source than the bespoke Pay-to-play_<Skill>_training wikitables.

Price layer (deterministic recompute): Module:GEPrices/data.json?action=raw (name-keyed JSON {item:price} + %LAST_UPDATE% ts) and/or the Real-time Prices API (prices.runescape.wiki/api/v1/osrs/latest|mapping|5m|1h|timeseries, id-keyed + volume + highalch).

Caveat: gather-vs-buy is DERIVED (empty inputs[] / gatherable materials = gather/ironman-friendly), not a labeled boolean. Account-type granularity is coarse in structured data: ironman appears as category text; HCIM/UIM viability is icon-based on the ironman guides; GIM lives only on Group_Ironman_Mode (prose+one table).
gaps: No single structured source labels 'GP/buy vs gather' as a boolean — you must DERIVE it. In the money_making_guide bucket: non-empty GP-valued inputs[] = buy/main cost; empty inputs[] = gather/free (ironman-friendly). In Module:Skill calc data: methods whose materials[] are self-gatherable = ironman path. Reliable but inferred, not a labeled field.

Account-type variants are not first-class in structured data. The bucket has no HCIM/UIM/GIM dimension ('Ironman' is only ad-hoc category text); Module:Skill calc is main-account only. UIM/HCIM viability on the ironman guides is encoded as inline chat-badge icon IMAGES (not parseable fields). GIM-specific economy (group trading caps, shared storage relaxing the no-GE constraint) is only on Group_Ironman_Mode as prose + one task table.

bucket.where() cannot substring/LIKE-filter the json blob (LIKE errors; exact-string where on json returns []). There is no server-side method-name filter — you must fetch all 627 rows and filter client-side. Plan ingest accordingly.

Per-method XP is not guaranteed in every money_making_guide bucket row (Module:Mmgtable routes XP through an internal handleXP() path). For authoritative per-method XP, prefer Module:Skill calc/<Skill> (xp field per action). Cross-check MMG rows that lack XP.

The Ironman Guide/<Skill>, Ultimate Ironman Guide/<Skill>, and top-level ironman/UIM hubs are prose-dominant; XP rates and gather-vs-buy distinctions are embedded in sentences. Low automated parseability — treat as supplementary/qualitative.

All profit/cost/GP-per-XP numbers depend on live GE prices. The bucket and Module:GEPrices/data.json are cache-refreshed (the latter carries %LAST_UPDATE%); values are point-in-time. Any DB ingest must record a fetch/price timestamp and re-poll, optionally recomputing exactly from Module:GEPrices/data.json or the Real-time Prices API. Module:GEPrices is name-keyed while the Real-time Prices API is id-keyed — joining requires the /mapping endpoint.

Some loadouts carry a 'style' label (e.g. Solo Melee, Magic (Barrage), Melee Tank).
canonical_pages.notes: NEW — discovered in the Bucket: namespace; the proposed map wrongly claimed gear recommendations have NO structured source. RE-VERIFIED LIVE: the queryable field is literally 'json' (NOT 'recommended_equipment'/'equipment' — those error); page_name is a valid field. CAVEAT on parseable=partial: the slot arrays contain wikitext link strings like '[[File:Graceful cape.png|...]][[Graceful cape|Graceful cape]]', so you must regex/parse the [[Item|Label]] links out. canonical_pages.title: Bucket API — Recipe bucket [structured PRODUCTION skill requirements]
canonical_pages.data_contained: Structured crafting/production recipes. Fields: uses_material (PAGE, repeated), uses_tool (PAGE, repeated), uses_facility (PAGE, repeated), uses_skill (PAGE, repeated), is_members_only (BOOLEAN), is_boostable (TEXT, repeated), source_template (TEXT), production_json (TEXT). production_json embeds skill name + required LEVEL + experience, e.g. "skills":[{"name":"Smithing","level":"1","experience":"6.2"}].
canonical_pages.notes: NEW — the closest thing to a STRUCTURED skill-level requirement anywhere on the wiki, but it is the level to PRODUCE/craft an item, not the level to WIELD it. Still highly relevant: for many gear items the make-level is the de-facto progression gate, and uses_skill/production_json give skill+level+xp as data. parseable=partial because the level lives inside the production_json TEXT blob and must be JSON-decoded. Does not solve wield requirements but materially shrinks the 'no structured requirements' gap for crafted gear.

canonical_pages.title: Bucket API — Exchange bucket [GE buy limits + alch, static]

best_machine_readable: Two complementary official APIs, both LIVE-VERIFIED (2026-06): (1) the OSRS Wiki Bucket API (action=bucket) for STATS — bucket('infobox_bonuses') joined with bucket('infobox_item'); the official replacement for the deprecated SMW action=ask and the same layer the wiki itself uses (Module:FetchItemStats); covers stats + slot + speed but NOT wield requirements. (2) the Real-time Prices API (https://prices.runescape.wiki/api/v1/osrs/) for PRICES + an item dictionary — /mapping returns id/name/members/limit/value/highalch/lowalch/examine/icon, and /latest|/5m|/1h|/timeseries give live and historical GE prices (the cost axis of gear progression). For machine-readable gear RECOMMENDATIONS use bucket('recommended_equipment').select('json','page_name') (per-activity slot->item-alternatives JSON, with wikilink strings to parse). Stats query: https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/api.php?action=bucket&format=json&query=bucket('infobox_bonuses').select('stab_attack_bonus','slash_attack_bonus','crush_attack_bonus','range_attack_bonus','magic_attack_bonus','stab_defence_bonus','slash_defence_bonus','crush_defence_bonus','range_defence_bonus','magic_defence_bonus','strength_bonus','ranged_strength_bonus','magic_damage_bonus','prayer_bonus','equipment_slot','weapon_attack_speed','weapon_attack_range','combat_style').run() . The one true gap remains WIELD/SKILL-TO-WIELD requirements, which have NO structured source and must be prose-extracted or sourced externally (RuneLite/cache item defs).
gaps: WIELD/SKILL-TO-WIELD REQUIREMENTS have no structured source. Confirmed absent from infobox_bonuses (18 fields) and infobox_item (17 fields, no 'type'/'equipable'/requirement field), from Template:Infobox Bonuses params, from Module:FetchItemStats, and from slot tables. RE-VERIFIED on Abyssal whip: the level is prose only ('requires an Attack level of 70 to wield'). The Recipe bucket gives skill levels to PRODUCE/craft (not to wield). To get wield requirements into a DB: regex/LLM-extract from article intros, OR source externally (RuneLite/cache item definitions), OR crowd-build.

GEAR PROGRESSION is only PARTIALLY machine-readable (correction to the proposed map). The recommended_equipment bucket DOES give structured per-activity/per-boss loadouts (slot->ordered item alternatives, with page_name and sometimes a style label) — but the items are wikitext link strings that must be parsed, and it is activity-scoped, not a tiered F2P->endgame path. The tiered narrative (Guide:*_Gear_Progression) and theoretical best-per-stat (Armour/Highest bonuses) remain prose/wikitable and are not a clean DAG.

NO ACCOUNT-TYPE STRUCTURED VARIANTS. recommended_equipment, the Guide: pages and Armour pages are all written for mains and do not split by account type. Ironman/HCIM/UIM/GIM gear advice exists only as separate prose guides (e.g. Ultimate Ironman Guide/Equipment). Account-type-aware BiS must be hand-curated/LLM-extracted.

BUCKET FIELD-NAME FOOTGUNS (expanded & re-verified): template params (astab, str, mdmg) != bucket columns (stab_attack_bonus, strength_bonus, magic_damage_bonus); infobox_item uses is_members_only (not 'members') and high_alchemy_value (not 'high_alch') and has NO 'type'/'equipable'/'slot' field; the recommended_equipment bucket's queryable field is 'json' (NOT 'recommended_equipment' or 'equipment'); a bare .run() with no .select() errors. Boolean-ish fields (is_members_only, tradeable) come back as ''/'true' strings, not JSON booleans — coerce. Always confirm against Bucket:<Name> / Special:AllPages?namespace=9592 before building queries.

MULTI-VERSION items (charged/uncharged, ornament kits, league variants) use version_anchor/default_version in infobox_item; ingestion must handle multiple rows per page name. Item-id fields are TEXT and 'repeated' (arrays) in the buckets, so a single page can map to multiple item ids — reconcile against the Real-time Prices /mapping id when joining the two APIs.

TWO ID SPACES TO RECONCILE: the Bucket layer (infobox_item.item_id, exchange.id) and the Real-time Prices /mapping id should agree but are sourced differently; verify the join key (prefer numeric id; fall back to canonical name) and handle items present in one source but not the other (untradeable items have no GE price).

best_machine_readable: OSRS Wiki Bucket API — table `combat_achievement`. Endpoint: https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/api.php?action=bucket&format=json&query=bucket('combat_achievement').select('id','name','monster','task','tier','type','league_region').limit(5000).run() — Returns JSON rows for every CA task with fields id, name, monster, task (description), tier, type, league_region. CONFIRMED LIVE (2026-06-16) via WebFetch: returned valid JSON, e.g. {id:1,name:"Abyssal Adept",monster:"Abyssal Sire",type:"Kill Count",tier:"Hard",task:"Kill the Abyssal Sire 20 times.",league_region:"General"}. This is the SAME store Module:Combat Achievements queries via bucket('combat_achievement') (verified: the module's Lua contains exactly this select() and limit(5000)). CRITICAL CAVEAT: you MUST pass an explicit .limit(5000) — the bucket has a low default row cap, so omitting limit silently truncates the result set (observed partial counts when limit was dropped). Point value is NOT a field — derive from tier in code: easy=1, medium=2, hard=3, elite=4, master=5, grandmaster=6 (this exact map is hard-coded in Module:Combat Achievements). completion% is a separate join: Module:Combat Achievements/completion.json, keyed by id.

This is THE authoritative structured store; the rendered wiki pages are all generated from it.

canonical_pages.title: Bucket API: money_making_guide bucket (action=bucket)
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED authoritative structured datastore for ALL money-making methods (~744 rows; more rows than unique pages because multi-Version methods produce one row each). The bucket has only THREE declared columns plus an implicit page_name: value (TEXT, profit gp/hr e.g. '803033.9154628'), recurring (BOOLEAN), json (TEXT, a stringified payload). NOTE: page_name, value, recurring, json are the ONLY top-level/filterable fields. The json TEXT payload (must be JSON-parsed client-side) contains: skillcategory, activity, members (bool), skill (RENDERED HTML with span/links, NOT clean level data), quest, category (Combat/Skilling/Processing/Collecting), intensity (Low/Moderate/High), isperkill (bool), inputs[] (name,value,qty,isph,pricetype), outputs[] (name,value,qty,isph,pricetype), and prices{ default_kph, input_perkill, output_perkill, output_perhour, input_perhour, default_value, kph_text }. Profit (value) = outputs - inputs using live GE prices after 2% GE tax (capped 5,000,000). XP/hr is NOT stored.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED SINGLE BEST ENTRY POINT. Live JSON via MediaWiki action=bucket; Module:Mmgtable writes every method via bucket('money_making_guide').put({value=outputs-inputs, recurring=bool, json=bucket_data}). CORRECTIONS vs proposal: (1) .where() ONLY works on the 4 top-level fields (page_name/value/recurring/json). You CANNOT filter on members/category/intensity/skill server-side because they live inside the single json TEXT blob; where('json.members',false) ERRORS with 'Bucket json not found in query.' So F2P/category filtering MUST be done client-side after JSON-parsing json. (2) recurring=false rows omit the field in select output (shows empty); to find recurring methods use .where('recurring', true). (3) value is TEXT, so numeric where('value','>',N) comparisons are unreliable/not honored — sort/filter profit client-side. (4) Default page size is 500 when no limit given; .limit()/.offset() pagination both verified working — page through with offset for a full pull. Profit is point-in-time (live GE).
