canonical_pages.title: Money making guide/Free-to-play (F2P rendered table)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Rendered wikitable of F2P-accessible methods, ranked by hourly profit. Columns: Method (linked), Hourly profit, Required skills, Category, Intensity, Members status. Cached prices. Intro: 'For a full list of all the money making methods, see Money making guide.'
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED & PROMOTED to its own entry. Because you CANNOT server-side filter the bucket on members, this rendered page is the cleanest ready-made F2P list. For programmatic F2P, instead pull the bucket and filter client-side on json.members==false. Other category sub-pages (/Skilling, /Combat, /Processing, /Collecting, /Recurring) have the same columns and are equally lossy.

canonical_pages.title: Individual method pages e.g. Money making guide/Killing green dragons
canonical_pages.data_contained: Per-method source page using {{Mmgtable}}. Full detail that populates one (or, with Version tabs, multiple) bucket rows: activity, image, skill/item/quest/other requirements, location, category, members, intensity, per-input/per-output rows (item, qty, value, note), kph, and a prose 'Details' section.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED. Authoring source for bucket rows. Parse the bucket instead of these wikitext pages. A page with a Version tab (e.g. 'Wilderness') yields multiple bucket rows — this is why total rows (~744) exceed unique page_names.

canonical_pages.title: Template:Mmgtable
canonical_pages.data_contained: Documents the authoring schema for a method: Activity, Version, Image, Skill, Item, Quest, Other, Other Benefits, Input#/num/value/note/isph, Output#/num/value/note, Location, Category, Members, Intensity, Details, kph, kph name, isperkill, Exclude. States profit is auto-calculated; Exclude=yes suppresses the bucket write.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED. Schema/field reference, not data. Best doc for understanding what each json field means.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Mmgtable (Lua renderer + bucket writer)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Lua implementation that renders the HTML table AND writes each method via bucket('money_making_guide').put({ value=parsedOutput.value-parsedInput.value, recurring=true/false, json=bucket_data }). VERIFIED tax logic: return val - math.min(math.floor(val * 0.02), 5000000) — i.e. 2% GE tax capped at 5,000,000 applied to outputs before profit subtraction.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED. The code that defines bucket population and gp/hr math (incl. GE tax). Confirms recurring is a real boolean column (put with recurring=false for normal methods, true for recurring ones). Reference for auditing/replicating the math; logic, not a data table.

canonical_pages.title: Ironman money making guide
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED: mostly prose (Early Money Making / Mid-Late Game Money Making / Alternative Money Making) plus a 'Money Making Tables' section with MANUALLY-maintained tables. The Recommended table columns: Method, Hourly Profit, Hourly XP, Hourly Resources, Requirements, Notes. The Low requirements and High requirements tables: Method, Hourly Profit, UIM badge, Requirements, Category, Description. UIM/HCIM badges and Yes/Maybe/No viability markers appear; Wilderness methods flagged risky for HCIM.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED GAP PAGE: NOT bucket-backed, NOT generated from Mmgtable — hand-maintained and inconsistent. Only light account-type signals (UIM/HCIM badges, ad-hoc notes). NO GIM coverage. Low-trust supplementary prose, not a structured source. (Note: unlike the bucket, the Recommended table here does include an Hourly XP column, but as free-text not structured data.)

gaps: Account-type profit is MAIN-account only: the bucket prices everything at live Grand Exchange buy/sell, so gp/hr is meaningful for mains but not for ironman/HCIM/UIM accounts (no GE access). There is no per-account-type profit field, and no GIM coverage anywhere structured.

NO server-side account-type or category filtering on the bucket: members, category, intensity, and skill all live inside the single json TEXT blob, not as queryable columns. where('json.members',false) ERRORS. You can only .where() on page_name/value/recurring/json. F2P vs members and category splits must be done client-side after JSON-parsing the json field (or by scraping the rendered /Free-to-play and category sub-pages).
value is stored as TEXT, so numeric where() comparisons (e.g. value > 1,000,000) are unreliable/not honored. Profit filtering and sorting must be done client-side after casting to number.

recurring=false rows omit the field in select results (returns empty string), so you cannot read recurring from a plain select; you must use .where('recurring', true) to identify recurring (per-day/passive) methods.

The json.skill field is RENDERED HTML (spans + wiki links), not clean structured level requirements. Extracting required skills+levels requires HTML parsing of that string; it is not first-class structured data. Quests (json.quest) are wiki-link text, also needing parsing.
XP/hr is NOT stored in the bucket json payload. The Ironman guide's Recommended table has an Hourly XP column but it is hand-entered free text, not structured.

Ironman/HCIM/UIM/GIM money-making has NO structured (bucket/module) source. The only resource is Ironman_money_making_guide: hand-maintained prose + manual tables (partial parse), with badge-level UIM/HCIM hints and zero GIM coverage. Ironman-viability (no GE inputs) is not a field anywhere — infer it from json.inputs[] (a method requiring bought inputs is generally not ironman-eligible).

Profit values are dynamic (recomputed against live GE prices each render/query), so any ingest is a point-in-time snapshot that drifts; rendered pages additionally serve CACHED prices. No historical/versioned profit series exists in the bucket.

Multi-version method rows: a page with a Version tab yields multiple bucket entries (total ~744 rows vs fewer unique page_names). page_name is NOT a unique key; but Version/version is inside the json blob (not a top-level column), so de-duplication keying on (page_name, json.version) requires parsing json.

Pagination required for a full pull: default page size is 500 rows when no limit is given. Use .limit()/.offset() to page (both verified working). Documented hard caps on limit() live on the WeirdGloop meta-wiki Extension:Bucket/Api page, which is outside oldschool.runescape.wiki and was not fetched under the single-domain constraint.
verify_changes: Verified all 10 URLs resolve; confirmed bucket schema is only value(TEXT)/recurring(BOOLEAN)/json(TEXT)+page_name; corrected the major false claim that you can .where() on members/category (they live in the json TEXT blob, so where('json.members',...) ERRORS — filtering must be client-side); fixed recurring (false rows omit the field, use where('recurring',true)); noted value is TEXT so numeric comparisons are unreliable; added default page size 500 + verified limit/offset pagination; flagged json.skill as rendered HTML (downgrades requirement parseability); added Bucket:Money_making_guide schema page, RuneScape:Bucket usage doc, and the F2P sub-page; confirmed GE tax = 2% capped 5,000,000 via Module:Mmgtable; added GIM gap; meta-wiki API-caps page left unfetched per single-domain restriction.

domain: Clue scrolls (Treasure Trails): tiers, step types, requirements, drop sources, and rewards

canonical_pages.title: Treasure Trails
canonical_pages.data_contained: Top-level hub and canonical index for the whole domain. VERIFIED section headings: 'Difficulty levels', 'Obtaining clues', 'Stacking limitations', 'Risks', 'Solving the Treasure Trails', 'Antagonists', 'STASH units', 'Rewards', 'Ranks', 'Changes', 'Trivia', 'References'. Contains: a 'Difficulty levels' wikitable with the 6 tiers and step-count ranges (beginner 1-3, easy 2-4, medium 3-5, hard 4-6, elite 5-7, master 6-8) plus links to per-tier guides/requirements/item-sources/rewards; a 'Solving the Treasure Trails' matrix of which step types each tier uses (anagrams, challenge scrolls, ciphers, coordinates, cryptic, emote, hot cold, light boxes, maps, puzzle boxes); an 'Antagonists' wikitable (combat NPCs at coordinate/emote steps, hard+); an 'Average rewards value' wikitable (expected gp per roll and per completed clue per tier); a 'Ranks' table (completion counts -> rank titles); a 'Milestone rewards' table (scroll case thresholds per tier).
canonical_pages.notes: Best human-readable overview and the canonical index. Mix of clean wikitables (difficulty levels, step-type matrix, antagonists, average value, ranks, milestones) and prose (obtaining/stacking/risks). Use ?action=raw for wikitext. Entry point to discover all sub-pages. All claimed tables CONFIRMED present.

canonical_pages.title: Clue scroll (hard) [representative of the per-tier item pages — use a MID tier, not master]
canonical_pages.data_contained: Per-tier item page. {{Infobox Item}} with structured fields (name, members=Yes, tradeable=No, examine, value=20, weight=0.02, release, equipable/stackable/noteable). States the tier step-count range. CRUCIAL: easy/medium/hard/elite pages carry an '==Item sources==' section built with the single template call {{Drop sources|Clue scroll (<tier>)}}, which now renders a Lua/Bucket-backed table of 150+ monster/activity sources with Source, Level, Quantity, Rarity, and League region columns. WARNING: the {{Infobox Item}} wikitext field '|id = N/A' for clue scrolls — the numeric item id (e.g. master 19835) is NOT reliably in the infobox source; get item_id from the Bucket infobox_item table instead.
canonical_pages.notes: One page per tier: Clue_scroll_(beginner/easy/medium/hard/elite/master). Infobox parseable via ?action=raw EXCEPT the id field is N/A in wikitext. {{Drop sources}} is the cleanest way to see drop sources, but its UNDERLYING DATA is now best obtained via the Bucket API (see Bucket entry), not by scraping. IMPORTANT EXCEPTION: Clue_scroll_(master) and Clue_scroll_(beginner) do NOT use {{Drop sources}} for monster drops — master clues come from reward caskets (hard 1/15, medium 1/30, easy 1/50, elite 1/5) and beginner from low-tier activities, so those two pages list a small manual 'Item sources' table instead. No account-type variants on the item.

canonical_pages.title: Reward casket (master) [representative of the 6 per-tier reward casket pages]
canonical_pages.data_contained: Per-tier REWARD tables — source of truth for clue rewards. Reward roll counts (master min 5/max 7/avg 6; elite 4/6/5; beginner 1/3/2). VERIFIED average loot value rendered on page (master ~120,971 gp per roll). Drop tables split into sections: tier uniques (incl. mega-rare gilded/3rd age table), standard table (weapons/runes/food/resources), shared treasure-trail items (cross-tier), tertiary (pets e.g. Bloodhound 1/1000, scroll sack/cases, master scroll, mimic). Each row has Item, Quantity (single or range), Rarity (per-roll fraction), GE price, High Alch.
canonical_pages.notes: One page per tier: Reward_casket_(beginner/easy/medium/hard/elite/master). Rows built with {{DropsTableHead}} / {{DropsLineReward|name=|quantity=|rarity=|altrarity=|raritynotes=|gemw=}} / {{DropsTableBottom}} (rendered via Module:DropsLine). VERIFIED: higher-tier pages (master, hard) use {{#expr}} inline for some rarities (e.g. rarity=1/{{#expr:1/(22/23*1/29) round 1}}); the beginner page uses plain fractions only (e.g. 1/360, 11/492) with NO #expr. Wikitext (?action=raw) is highly structured. Module:DropsLine ALSO writes each row to the Bucket:Dropsline data store (drop_json), the API-queryable layer — NOT Semantic MediaWiki (SMW is hard-deprecated).

canonical_pages.title: Bucket API (action=bucket) — Bucket:Dropsline + Bucket:Infobox item [MOST machine-parseable layer; supersedes SMW]
canonical_pages.data_contained: The OSRS Wiki's official structured-data API. Returns JSON. Bucket:Dropsline schema columns = item_name (PAGE), drop_json (TEXT), rare_drop_table (BOOLEAN); the drop_json blob contains per-row sub-fields: 'Dropped item', 'Dropped from', 'Rarity', 'Alt Rarity', 'Drop Quantity', 'Quantity High/Low', 'Drop Value', 'Drop level', 'Drop type', 'Rolls', 'League region', 'Rarity Notes'. VERIFIED working: querying item_name='Clue scroll (hard)' returns records like {'Dropped from':'Green dragon#Regular','Rarity':'1/128','Drop Quantity':'1'}. Also Bucket:Infobox item holds item_id/image/examine etc. (query with select('item_id','image','examine').where('item_name',...)).
canonical_pages.notes: Query syntax: api.php?action=bucket&format=json&query=bucket('<table>').select('<f1>','<f2>').where('<field>','<value>').limit(N).run(); all bucket/field names lowercase with underscores. CONFIRMED: bare action=ask / askargs return badvalue ('Unrecognized value for parameter action: ask') — SMW JSON ask is unavailable AND SMW is officially hard-deprecated/scheduled for removal; Bucket is its replacement. This is the authoritative way to enumerate clue drop sources without scraping. Caveat: field names differ from intuition (e.g. infobox_item has no 'members' field; dropsline detail lives inside drop_json, not as top-level selectable columns) — fetch the schema page (Bucket:Dropsline) first. Docs: RuneScape:Bucket and Help:Editing/Bucket.

canonical_pages.title: Treasure Trails/Full guide/All (+ per-tier Full guides and granular Treasure Trails/Guide/<StepType>/<Tier> subpages)
canonical_pages.data_contained: The actual clue STEPS: clue text/puzzle, solution/answer, location, requirements, and whether a fight is triggered. VERIFIED organized by step type (anagrams, ciphers, coordinates, cryptic, emote, maps, hot/cold, Sherlock challenges, puzzle/light boxes) then by tier. Data physically lives in granular transcluded subpages e.g. Treasure_Trails/Guide/Cryptic_clues/Master (VERIFIED ~30 master cryptic rows), /Anagrams/Master, /Emote_clues/Hard.
canonical_pages.notes: Each step is a consistent template call ({{Cryptics}}, {{Anagrams}}, {{Emotes}}, {{Coordinates}}). Templated rows are machine-readable via ?action=raw of the per-step-type/per-tier subpages, BUT the 'Notes' field bundles location + requirements + instructions as free prose with wikilinks (VERIFIED: e.g. master cryptic note 'Dig in front of the spinning light in Ping and Pong's room inside the Iceberg. A clockwork suit is required...'), so requirements/locations are NOT cleanly separated columns. Per-tier full guides also exist: Treasure_Trails/Full_guide/{Beginner..Master} (beginner also at Beginner_clue_scroll_guide). Best for step inventory; needs NLP for structured requirements. Coordinate subpages are slightly more structured (quest/skill requirement + combat columns).

canonical_pages.title: Module:DropsLine / Template:DropsLine / Module:Get drop info / Template:Drop sources (drop-row engine + Bucket backing)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Documents the drop-row engine. {{DropsTableHead}}/{{DropsLineReward}}/{{DropsLine}}/{{DropsTableBottom}} are rendered by Module:DropsLine, which writes each row into the Bucket:Dropsline data store as drop_json (fields: Dropped item, Dropped from, Rarity, Alt Rarity, Drop Quantity, Drop Value, Drop level, Drop type, Rolls, League region...). {{Drop sources}} invokes Module:Get drop info, which READS Bucket:Dropsline (drop_json) to list every source dropping a given item. This is the mechanism making both the Reward casket pages and the {{Drop sources}} sections machine-readable AND API-queryable.
canonical_pages.notes: Not a data page itself but the schema/engine. Knowing DropsLineReward params (name, quantity, rarity, altrarity, raritynotes, gemw) lets you parse reward casket wikitext deterministically; knowing the Bucket:Dropsline schema lets you query the same data via the Bucket API instead of scraping. CORRECTED from prior map: the backing store is the Bucket system (Bucket:Dropsline), NOT Semantic MediaWiki — SMW properties are deprecated. No standalone Module: holds all clue data as one Lua table; clue data is distributed across per-tier item, reward casket, and guide subpages, but drop/reward rows are unified in Bucket:Dropsline.

best_machine_readable: Use the OSRS Wiki Bucket API (api.php?action=bucket&format=json) as the primary structured layer — it is official, returns JSON, and supersedes the now-hard-deprecated Semantic MediaWiki (bare action=ask returns badvalue). For DROP SOURCES per tier: bucket('dropsline').where('item_name','Clue scroll (<tier>)') — VERIFIED returns records with 'Dropped from'/'Rarity'/'Drop Quantity' inside drop_json (note: easy/medium/hard/elite only; master and beginner are casket/activity-obtained, not in the monster-drop set). For ITEM METADATA (id, examine, value): bucket('infobox_item').where('item_name','Clue scroll (<tier>)') (use the schema's actual field names). For REWARDS: the six Reward_casket_(<tier>) pages — every reward row is a {{DropsLineReward|name=|quantity=|rarity=}} call (Module:DropsLine), mirrored into Bucket:Dropsline as drop_json; ?action=raw works but resolve {{#expr}} rarities (present on master/hard, absent on beginner). For TIERS / step-type matrix / average values: Treasure_Trails clean wikitables. For STEP CONTENT: the templated Treasure_Trails/Guide/<StepType>/<Tier> subpages via ?action=raw (Notes field is prose, needs NLP). Single overall entry point = Treasure_Trails; single most-parseable dataset for drops/rewards = the Bucket API (dropsline), with reward-casket wikitext as the human-auditable fallback.
gaps: No single Lua Module holds all clue-scroll data as one structured table. Data is distributed across per-tier item pages, per-tier reward casket pages, and many per-step-type/per-tier guide subpages — but drop/reward ROWS are unified and API-queryable via Bucket:Dropsline.

Step REQUIREMENTS (quests/skills/items per step) are embedded as free prose inside the 'Notes' field of step templates ({{Cryptics}}, {{Coordinates}}, etc.), not discrete machine fields — extracting them cleanly needs NLP; step-level requirements are only 'partial' parseable. Coordinate subpages are slightly more structured.
Per-tier step-count is given only as a RANGE (e.g. master 6-8), not a deterministic per-individual-clue count (correct game behaviour).

The Bucket API field names are non-obvious: dropsline exposes only item_name/drop_json/rare_drop_table as selectable columns (drop detail is nested inside the drop_json TEXT blob and must be JSON-parsed after fetch); infobox_item lacks an intuitive 'members' field. Always fetch the Bucket schema page (Bucket:Dropsline) before building a query.

CORRECTION vs prior map: master and beginner clue item pages do NOT enumerate monster drops via {{Drop sources}} — master is casket-sourced (hard 1/15, medium 1/30, easy 1/50, elite 1/5) and beginner is low-tier-activity-sourced, so the {{Drop sources}}/Bucket dropsline source-enumeration only applies to easy/medium/hard/elite.
CORRECTION vs prior map: the {{Infobox Item}} wikitext for clue scrolls has |id = N/A — the numeric item id is not in the infobox source; obtain item_id from Bucket:Infobox item instead.

Reward rarities are per-roll fractions and some are computed inline with {{#expr}} (verified on master/hard; beginner uses plain fractions) plus altrarity/raritynotes conditionals — a parser of the wikitext must resolve expressions/conditionals; the Bucket drop_json gives resolved Rarity/Alt Rarity strings, avoiding this.

No account-type (main/ironman/HCIM/UIM/GIM) variants exist for clue tiers, steps, drop sources, or reward tables. Clue data is account-type-agnostic; account-type differences are only in tradeability/death-mechanics (separate pages). Mimic and scroll case/sack are alternate reward paths, not account-type variants. Verified no missing account-type variant pages to add.
verify_changes: Replaced the outdated 'SMW via Special:Ask only' framing with the live, JSON-returning Bucket API (action=bucket; SMW is hard-deprecated) and verified a working dropsline query as the most machine-parseable drop/reward source; swapped the representative item page from master to hard because master/beginner clues are casket/activity-sourced (NOT {{Drop sources}}-backed); fixed that the infobox id field is N/A in wikitext (use Bucket:Infobox item for item_id); and confirmed all Treasure_Trails tables, the DropsLineReward/#expr structure (present on master/hard, absent on beginner), and that no account-type variants exist.

canonical_pages.title: Minigames (hub page)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Authoritative human-curated overview of all minigames, split into Combat, Skilling, Combat-and-skilling, and Minigame-like bosses. Columns: League region, Minigame, Minigame classification (Safe/Dangerous, Non-combat), Skill(s), Description, Rewards (descriptive). Best single page for the canonical taxonomy/membership of which activities count as minigames.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED: page renders as hardcoded HTML wikitables (separate tables for Combat / Skilling / Combat-and-skilling / Minigame-like bosses, with a League region first column). Columns are descriptive prose, not atomic fields: no per-minigame level-requirements column, rewards in free text. Parseable as HTML tables for list+classification+skills; requirements/rewards must be enriched elsewhere. Hand-maintained, so can drift from per-page infoboxes — prefer Category + infobox_activity bucket for membership truth. The {{Minigames}} navbox is navigation only.

canonical_pages.title: Category:Minigames
canonical_pages.data_contained: Flat alphabetical membership list of ~52 minigame articles (Barbarian Assault, Castle Wars, The Gauntlet, Pest Control, TzHaar Fight Cave, etc.) plus ~48 subcategories. Cleanest authoritative enumeration of 'what is a minigame'.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED: 52 articles + 48 subcategories; the parent 'Minigames' page is itself included as the first entry (filter it out). Best machine-readable way to get the canonical SET of titles: action=query&list=categorymembers&cmtitle=Category:Minigames&cmlimit=max&format=json. Titles only; no requirements/rewards — hydrate from infobox wikitext + storeline bucket. Includes some boss-like activities, so light filtering needed.

canonical_pages.title: Bucket:Infobox activity (api.php?action=bucket)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Structured-data store populated by every {{Infobox Activity}} transclusion (~99 entries, VERIFIED live). Live-queryable: api.php?action=bucket&format=json&query=bucket('infobox_activity').select('page_name','is_members_only').run() returns JSON list of activities/minigames with membership status.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED live (99 entries returned; map previously said 98 — minor drift). BEST machine-readable entry point for list + members flag as JSON. CONFIRMED THIN SCHEMA: the bucket stores ONLY page_name, page_name_sub, image, is_members_only, default_version — selecting 'location' returns 'Field location not found in bucket infobox_activity'. So no requirements/skills/currency/location are bucketed here. CAVEAT: is_members_only is empty for many entries in live results, so treat the members flag as unreliable/incomplete — cross-check the per-page infobox 'members' param.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Infobox Activity (Lua source of truth) + Template:Infobox Activity
canonical_pages.data_contained: Lua module (invoked by Template:Infobox Activity) that defines the per-minigame infobox: which params exist, the activity 'type' enum (Minigame / Distraction and Diversion / Raid), and what is written to the infobox_activity bucket. Template params: name, image, release, update, removal, aka, type, members, location, players, skills, requirements, currency, tutorial, music, map, leagueRegion, dropversion (plus version#/defver/bucketname/bucket). Holds per-minigame requirements/skills/currency in the wikitext.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED: Template:Infobox Activity invokes Module:Infobox Activity (deps: Module:Infobox, Module:Mainonly, Module:Trailblazer Region). The module is the authoritative definition of the param set + which fields bucket (only members/image/default_version/page_name_sub). Per-minigame requirements/skills/currency live as free-text/wikilinks in each page's {{Infobox Activity}} wikitext and are NOT bucketed — to extract them you must fetch each page's ?action=raw and parse the params. Reference Module:Infobox_Activity to detect schema changes; use Template:Infobox_Activity/doc for the param list.

canonical_pages.title: Reward shops via Template:StoreLine → 'storeline' Bucket (api.php?action=bucket)
canonical_pages.data_contained: The standardized reward-shop tables ({{StoreTableHead}} + {{StoreLine}} rows) on minigame pages and dedicated reward-shop pages (Barbarian Assault, Soul Wars Reward Shop, Dom Onion's Reward Shop, etc.). Each row = one purchasable reward. CONFIRMED bucketed and live-queryable as bucket 'storeline' with rich fields: sold_by (PAGE), sold_item, sold_item_image, store_buy_price, store_sell_price, store_currency, store_delta, store_stock, store_buy_multiplier, store_sell_multiplier, restock_time, store_notes, sold_item_json (~6,237 rows wiki-wide).
canonical_pages.notes: CORRECTED + UPGRADED: the proposed map said StoreLine writes to a 'store' bucket — that is WRONG (api returns 'Bucket store does not exist', and Bucket:Store 404s). The real bucket is 'storeline' (one word). VERIFIED rich schema above and live filtering: bucket('storeline').select('sold_by','sold_item','store_buy_price','store_currency').where('store_currency','Zeal Tokens').run() returns Soul Wars Reward Shop rows. BEST machine-readable source for minigame REWARDS (item + cost + currency) as JSON. NOTE: currency string must match exactly (e.g. 'Zeal Tokens' works; 'Honour points' returned empty — BA may use a different label or sold_by, so discover values per shop). StoreTableHead sets sellmultiplier/buymultiplier/delta, NOT currency; currency is per-StoreLine and lands in store_currency.

canonical_pages.title: Currencies
canonical_pages.data_contained: Cross-reference of all OSRS currencies including minigame reward currencies (Honour points, Zeal Tokens/Soul Wars, Pizazz points/Mage Training Arena, Castle wars tickets, etc.). Tables (Tradeable/Untradeable/Fare/Virtual) with columns Currency, Location, Example usage — maps each minigame currency to its activity.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED: 4 sections (Tradeable/Untradeable/Fare/Virtual), 3-column tables (Currency, Location, Example usage); minigame reward currencies sit in the Virtual section. Useful JOIN table to link a minigame to its reward-currency string, which then keys into the storeline bucket's store_currency field (use the exact label seen there). Parseable as HTML tables, not a single consolidated machine schema.

canonical_pages.title: Ultimate Ironman Guide/Minigames (account-type variant guidance)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Prose guidance on minigame viability/restrictions for UIM accounts, organized as 'Death in Minigames' (item-loss risk, e.g. The Gauntlet) and 'Item Limitations' (Barbarian Assault, Castle Wars, Rogues' Den, Trouble Brewing). Companion to per-page warning boxes.
canonical_pages.notes: VERIFIED prose, no structured tables. This is the ONLY dedicated minigames subpage for any account type: the general Ironman_Guide has NO /Minigames subpage — only scattered prose mentions + a 'Limitations' section (HCIM follow same restrictions). There is NO structured/bucketed field capturing account-type restrictions per minigame anywhere; must be hand-curated or NLP-extracted. HCIM/GIM danger notes appear inline on individual minigame pages.

best_machine_readable: For the minigame LIST + (incomplete) members status: query the infobox_activity Bucket live — https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/api.php?action=bucket&format=json&query=bucket('infobox_activity').select('page_name','is_members_only').run() (VERIFIED, 99 entries; note is_members_only is empty for many rows, so cross-check the per-page infobox 'members' param). Enumerate/cross-check against Category:Minigames via action=query&list=categorymembers. For REWARDS (the big upgrade): query the 'storeline' Bucket (NOT 'store', which does not exist) — bucket('storeline').select('sold_by','sold_item','store_buy_price','store_sell_price','store_currency','store_stock').where('store_currency','<currency>').run() — VERIFIED returning item-level rewards+prices by currency (e.g. 'Zeal Tokens' → Soul Wars). Discover the exact store_currency label per shop (some differ from the Currencies page wording). For REQUIREMENTS/skills/currency: parse the {{Infobox Activity}} params from each page's ?action=raw (NOT bucketed); quest prereqs and many level requirements are only in body prose, not the infobox.
gaps: No single machine-readable table contains list + requirements + rewards together — data is split across Category (list), infobox_activity Bucket (list + partial members), per-page Infobox Activity wikitext (requirements/skills/currency), and the storeline Bucket (rewards).

The infobox_activity Bucket schema is thin: VERIFIED to store only page_name, page_name_sub, image, is_members_only, default_version. Valuable fields (location, requirements, skills, currency) are in the infobox display but NOT bucketed — selecting them errors 'Field not found'. Requirements must be scraped from raw wikitext per page.
infobox_activity's is_members_only is empty/unpopulated for many entries in the live bucket, so the members flag from this bucket is unreliable; cross-check the per-page infobox 'members' param.

Per-minigame 'requirements' infobox values are unnormalized free text/wikilinks; many requirements are NOT in the infobox at all — VERIFIED on Guardians of the Rift, where the level 27 Runecraft requirement and the Temple of the Eye quest prereq appear only in body prose (the infobox has no 'requirements' param).

storeline rewards are queryable by currency but the store_currency label must match exactly — 'Zeal Tokens' works while 'Honour points' returned empty, so currency/shop labels must be discovered per shop (via Currencies page or by querying storeline grouped by sold_by) rather than assumed.

Account-type variants (ironman/HCIM/UIM/GIM) are entirely prose: only Ultimate Ironman Guide/Minigames is a dedicated subpage (the general Ironman Guide has no /Minigames subpage); plus warning boxes/changelog notes per page. NO structured field per minigame for account-type restrictions — requires manual curation or NLP.
'Unlocks' (Void armour, Fire cape, Infernal cape, etc.) have no single field; derive from storeline reward rows + body prose.
verify_changes: Corrected the reward source: the claimed 'store' Bucket does NOT exist (verified 404 + API error); the real bucket is 'storeline' with a rich verified schema (sold_by/sold_item/store_buy_price/store_sell_price/store_currency/store_stock/restock_time, ~6,237 rows) and confirmed live currency filtering — upgrading rewards from a vague template claim to a true machine-readable API source; added Module:Infobox Activity as the Lua source-of-truth; fixed infobox_activity schema (adds page_name_sub) and flagged is_members_only as often empty/unreliable; noted StoreTableHead sets multipliers not currency; tightened account-type note (no general Ironman/Minigames subpage); all other listed URLs fetched and confirmed to resolve and match their claimed shape.

best_machine_readable: https://prices.runescape.wiki/api/v1/osrs/latest (the live price feed) paired with https://prices.runescape.wiki/api/v1/osrs/mapping (item ID -> name/limit/alch metadata join table). Documented at https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/w/RuneScape:Real-time_Prices. This is the single best entry point for live GE prices: one GET returns all tradeable items as JSON keyed by numeric item ID with high/low instant prices and unix timestamps. ALL endpoint shapes re-verified live on 2026-06-16: /latest?id=4151 returned {"data":{"4151":{"high,highTime,low,lowTime}}}; /mapping is a JSON ARRAY of objects with exactly {examine,id,members,lowalch,limit,value,highalch,icon,name}; /5m is {"data":{id:{avgHighPrice,avgLowPrice,highPriceVolume,lowPriceVolume}}} (the wiki's prose docs page loosely calls these high/low/highVolume/lowVolume, but the live JSON uses avgHighPrice/avgLowPrice/highPriceVolume/lowPriceVolume); /timeseries?id&timestep returns {"data":[{timestamp,avgHighPrice,avgLowPrice,highPriceVolume,lowPriceVolume}],"itemId":id}. Use /5m or /1h for smoothed prices + volume when an instant price is too noisy or thin for a cost estimate. Requires a descriptive custom User-Agent (default client UAs are blocked); no auth.

canonical_pages.title: RuneScape:Real-time Prices (API documentation)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Official human-readable documentation for the OSRS Wiki / RuneLite real-time prices API. RE-FETCHED & CONFIRMED: documents base URLs (prices.runescape.wiki/api/v1/osrs for OSRS, /api/v1/dmm for Deadman Armageddon), all endpoints (/latest, /latest?id=, /mapping, /5m, /5m?timestamp=, /1h, /1h?timestamp=, /timeseries?id=&timestep={5m|1h|6h|24h}), JSON response shapes, the required descriptive custom User-Agent header, the list of pre-emptively BLOCKED default User-Agents (python-requests, Python-urllib, Apache-HttpClient, RestSharp, Java/{version}, curl/{version}), and usage etiquette ('we do not explicitly ratelimit' but discretionary blocking for sustained multiple-large-queries-per-second). This is the spec; the data lives on the prices.runescape.wiki API host.
canonical_pages.notes: Prose guide describing the canonical machine-readable JSON API. Treat the documented endpoints as the source of truth. CAVEAT FOUND ON RE-FETCH: this prose page describes the /5m & /1h fields loosely (as high/low/highVolume/lowVolume); the ACTUAL live JSON uses avgHighPrice/avgLowPrice/highPriceVolume/lowPriceVolume (verified directly against the endpoints). Trust the live endpoint field names over this page's prose. Page self-describes the project as 'fairly new' with possible downtime and prices that 'may not be 100% accurate'.

canonical_pages.title: Real-time Prices API — /latest endpoint (live JSON)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Live instant buy/sell prices for ALL tradeable items, keyed by numeric item ID. Each entry: {high, highTime, low, lowTime} where high=most recent instant-sell (insta-buy) price, low=most recent instant-buy (insta-sell) price, with unix timestamps. high/low may be null and items NEVER traded are simply ABSENT from the response (confirmed in wiki docs). RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-16: GET ?id=4151 returned {"data":{"4151":{"high":...,"highTime":...,"low":...,"lowTime":...}}} — shape unchanged. THE live price feed for cost computation.
canonical_pages.notes: Single GET returns the entire tradeable-item universe in one large JSON object keyed by item ID — do NOT loop per-id. Requires a descriptive custom User-Agent (default client UAs blocked) or the request is rejected. No auth. Updated event-driven/near-real-time (a value is only as fresh as the last real trade for that item). Top-level wrapper is {"data":{...}}.
account_type_variants: main (standard shared game economy; identical prices for all account types)

canonical_pages.title: Real-time Prices API — /mapping endpoint (item metadata join table)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Static-ish metadata for every tradeable item. RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-16: top level is a JSON ARRAY of objects; each object has exactly {examine, id, members(bool), lowalch, limit (GE 4-hour buy limit), value (store/base value), highalch, icon (wiki filename), name}. The JOIN table to turn numeric item IDs from /latest into names + GE buy limits + alch values for cost/profit math.
canonical_pages.notes: Essential companion to /latest, which is keyed only by numeric ID and has NO names. /mapping is required to resolve names and GE buy limits (critical for bulk-buy cost computation). Cache it (changes only on game updates). Same custom User-Agent requirement. NOTE: top level is an array, not an object — index/scan it, don't key it directly.

canonical_pages.title: Real-time Prices API — averaged endpoints (/5m, /1h)
canonical_pages.data_contained: /5m and /1h give time-averaged prices + volume per item over the window. RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-16: shape is {"data":{itemId:{avgHighPrice, avgLowPrice, highPriceVolume, lowPriceVolume}}} (fields can be null when no trades at that level). Both accept an optional ?timestamp=<unix> to fetch a specific past window. Use these for smoothed/representative prices and to gauge liquidity (volume) so a cost estimate isn't skewed by a single outlier trade.
canonical_pages.notes: CORRECTED field names confirmed against the live endpoint: avgHighPrice/avgLowPrice/highPriceVolume/lowPriceVolume (NOT the high/low/highVolume/lowVolume the wiki prose docs imply). The volume fields are the only place in the live API to assess whether an item is liquid enough for a planned bulk purchase. Without ?timestamp, returns the current window (no top-level timestamp key observed). Same User-Agent rule.

canonical_pages.title: Real-time Prices API — /timeseries endpoint (historical per-item)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Up to 365 historical data points for ONE item at a chosen timestep. RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-16: shape is {"data":[{timestamp, avgHighPrice, avgLowPrice, highPriceVolume, lowPriceVolume}, ...], "itemId": <id>}. timestep accepts 5m|1h|6h|24h. Use for trend/representativeness checks and to confirm an item's price isn't a transient spike before committing a bulk-buy cost estimate.
canonical_pages.notes: Split out from /5m & /1h for accuracy: /timeseries is PER-ID (id is required) and is the one endpoint where per-id is correct. Top-level carries both a data array and an itemId echo. Same User-Agent rule. Same /dmm variant available.

canonical_pages.title: Module:GEPrices/data.json (wiki guide-price bulk snapshot) — raw JSON
canonical_pages.data_contained: Flat JSON object mapping item NAME -> current GE guide price in gp (e.g. "Abyssal ashes":1739, "3rd age amulet":48606494). The wiki's own periodic-snapshot price cache that feeds {{GEP}}/{{GEPrice}} templates and Module:Exchange. RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-16 via ?action=raw: returns PURE JSON (no HTML wrapper) beginning {"%LAST_UPDATE%":1781603131,"%LAST_UPDATE_F%":"16 June 2026 09:45:31 (UTC)", ...}. Periodic (~daily), NOT real-time.
canonical_pages.notes: IMPROVED URL: use ?action=raw to get clean JSON directly instead of scraping the /w/ HTML page. Good FALLBACK / offline-cache and useful for name-keyed lookups, but it is a guide-price snapshot, not live, and lags the API. Two synthetic metadata keys (%LAST_UPDATE%, %LAST_UPDATE_F%) sit alongside the item entries — strip/skip them when parsing. Prefer the real-time API for cost computation; use this only if you need the exact value the wiki itself displays or want a name-keyed bulk file without ID resolution.

canonical_pages.title: Module:GEVolumes/data.json (wiki daily trade-volume snapshot) — raw JSON
canonical_pages.data_contained: Flat JSON object mapping item NAME -> daily GE trade volume (e.g. "Abyssal whip":4336, "Dragon bones":2898782, "Prayer potion(4)":1193604). NEWLY ADDED & VERIFIED 2026-06-16: ~5,000+ items, name-keyed, same %LAST_UPDATE% header (16 June 2026 09:45:31 UTC) as GEPrices — i.e. the periodic-snapshot liquidity companion to GEPrices.
canonical_pages.notes: Name-keyed liquidity snapshot to pair with Module:GEPrices when working off the wiki snapshot instead of the live API. For live work prefer the volume fields in /5m, /1h, /timeseries. Same %LAST_UPDATE% metadata keys to strip when parsing.

canonical_pages.title: Module:GEIDs/data.json (wiki name->itemID map) — raw JSON
canonical_pages.data_contained: Flat JSON object mapping item NAME -> numeric item/exchange ID (e.g. "3rd age amulet":10344, "3rd age axe":20011, "3rd age bow":12424). NEWLY ADDED & VERIFIED 2026-06-16: ~4,000+ entries, same %LAST_UPDATE% header. The name<->ID join table on the wiki side.
canonical_pages.notes: Useful ONLY if you build a name-keyed pipeline off the wiki Module snapshots (GEPrices/GEVolumes are name-keyed) and later need to cross to numeric IDs. For the live-API pipeline this is REDUNDANT: API /mapping already supplies id<->name plus limit and alch values, which GEIDs does not. Strip the %LAST_UPDATE% keys when parsing.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Exchange + per-item Module:Exchange/{ItemName} subpages
canonical_pages.data_contained: Module:Exchange is the Lua helper powering GE price display; it pulls bulk current prices from Module:GEPrices/data.json, volume from Module:GEVolumes/data.json, IDs from Module:GEIDs/data.json, and per-item metadata from Module:Exchange/{ItemName}. RE-VERIFIED a per-item subpage (Module:Exchange/Abyssal_whip): fields itemId=4151, item='Abyssal whip' (note: field is 'item', NOT 'name'), icon='Abyssal whip.png', value=120001, limit=70, members=true, category=nil, examine='A weapon from the Abyss.', hialch=72000, lowalch=48000. Per-item subpages hold STATIC metadata (limit/alch/value), NOT a live price.
canonical_pages.notes: Per-item subpages are Lua tables (parseable only if you evaluate Lua or scrape the rendered page — ?action=raw returns Lua source, not JSON), and they DUPLICATE metadata already in the API /mapping endpoint while lacking live prices. Lower value than the API for a database pipeline. Field is 'item' not 'name', and 'value' (120001) is a base/store value, not a market price — do not mistake it for a GE price. Module:Exchange itself is logic, not data.

gaps: The live API /latest is keyed ONLY by numeric item ID — it has no names. You MUST fetch and cache /mapping (a JSON ARRAY, not an object) separately to resolve IDs to names and GE buy limits. Two requests, two shapes to join.

Default HTTP-client User-Agents are BLOCKED (python-requests, Python-urllib, Apache-HttpClient, RestSharp, Java/{version}, curl/{version}). The pipeline must set a descriptive custom User-Agent with contact info (e.g. 'gilded-tome price sync - contact <email/discord>') or every request is rejected. No formal rate limit, but sustained multiple-large-queries-per-second risks discretionary blocking — cache aggressively.

No account-type variants for prices: the GE economy is shared, so main/ironman/HCIM/UIM/GIM all see identical guide/market prices. BUT all ironman-family accounts (Ironman, Hardcore Ironman, Ultimate Ironman, Group Ironman) CANNOT use the Grand Exchange in-game, so 'cost in GE gp' is informational/relative-value only for them — the planner must flag that ironmen acquire items via gameplay, not purchase (Group Ironman has shared group storage but still no GE access). This is a domain/account-type caveat, not a separate data source the wiki provides.

high/low can be null, and items NEVER traded are simply ABSENT from /latest (confirmed in wiki docs) — not just null. Thin-volume items give unreliable single-trade prices. Mitigate with /5m or /1h averages and the volume fields, but some illiquid items have no trustworthy live price at all.

Update cadence of /latest is event-driven and not formally documented (a value is only as fresh as the last real trade for that item). The wiki page self-describes the project as 'fairly new' with possible downtime and prices that 'may not be 100% accurate'.

Untradeable / non-GE items (quest items, untradeables, etc.) have NO entry in any of these sources — cost for those must come from a different domain (shop prices / production cost / drop value), not GE prices.

The wiki's own Module:GEPrices/GEVolumes/GEIDs snapshots are periodic (~daily; all stamped 16 June 2026 09:45:31 UTC at audit time) and name-keyed, so they will not agree exactly with the live API. Pick ONE as the canonical store (recommend the live API) and document the discrepancy. The %LAST_UPDATE% and %LAST_UPDATE_F% keys are mixed into the same object as item entries — strip them before treating the object as a pure name->value map.

The /5m & /1h field names in the wiki's PROSE docs page (high/low/highVolume/lowVolume) do not match the live JSON (avgHighPrice/avgLowPrice/highPriceVolume/lowPriceVolume) — always trust the live endpoint over the docs prose.
verify_changes: Re-fetched all listed URLs live (2026-06-16): confirmed /latest, /mapping (array), /5m, /timeseries shapes and exact field names; CORRECTED the /5m & /1h field names (avgHighPrice/avgLowPrice/highPriceVolume/lowPriceVolume — wiki prose docs were loose); split /timeseries into its own entry with verified {data[],itemId} shape; upgraded the wiki Module snapshot URLs to ?action=raw (returns pure JSON, strictly more parseable); ADDED verified companion data pages Module:GEVolumes/data.json (name->volume) and Module:GEIDs/data.json (name->id); fixed Module:Exchange subpage field name ('item' not 'name', value=base not market) and re-rated it partial; labeled the second economy 'Deadman Armageddon' (/api/v1/dmm) and propagated dmm variants to /5m & /timeseries; sharpened ironman-family GE-inaccessibility and 'never-traded items are absent (not null)' caveats. To re-verify later: confirm /latest shape is still {data:{id:{high,highTime,low,lowTime}}} (values change each run — that's live; a changed SHAPE signals a breaking change); confirm /mapping objects still carry id/name/limit/highalch/lowalch/value; diff the documented endpoint list at /w/RuneScape:Real-time_Prices for added/removed endpoints or new UA/rate rules; check the %LAST_UPDATE_F% header on the Module ?action=raw pages to confirm the snapshots still refresh.

domain: Wiki machine-readable layer (Module:/Lua data namespace, calculators, structured data / Bucket query API)
best_machine_readable: The OSRS Wiki Bucket API is the single best machine-readable entry point for structured ENTITY data (items, equipment bonuses, monsters/NPCs, combat achievements, etc.). Use the official docs page https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/w/RuneScape:Bucket as the canonical reference (it defines the query syntax and confirms it replaces the now hard-deprecated SMW action=ask), and issue live queries at https://oldschool.runescape.wiki/api.php?action=bucket (syntax: bucket('infobox_item').select(...).where(...).run() — all bucket/field names lowercase_with_underscores). For PROGRESSION-LOGIC data (XP curve, quest/diary requirements, training-method XP rates) the best entry point is the Module: namespace fetched raw via ...?action=raw (e.g. Module:Questreq/data, Module:Experience/data, Module:Skill calc/<Skill>), enumerable via the MediaWiki API (action=query&list=allpages&apnamespace=828). For account-type detection use Module:User hiscores (maps account-type aliases to Hiscores tables). For live GE prices use the prices.runescape.wiki API. In short: Bucket API for entity stats (items, equipment bonuses, combat achievements), Module raw-fetch for progression logic + collection log, prices.runescape.wiki for live prices.

canonical_pages.title: RuneScape:Bucket — official docs for the Bucket query system (canonical reference)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Official documentation for the Bucket extension: query syntax (select(), where(), run()), the rule that all bucket and field names must be lowercase_with_underscores, the location of the bucket list (Special:AllPages namespace 9592), and the statement that SMW (action=ask) is hard-deprecated and slated for removal. Points to authoritative technical docs at meta.weirdgloop.org. VERIFIED live, last modified 21 August 2025.
canonical_pages.notes: This is the human-readable spec page (prose+example), not a data endpoint itself — pair it with api.php?action=bucket for actual queries. Added in this audit as the more authoritative canonical reference than the bare action=bucket endpoint. Quote: 'This is hard deprecated, and will be removed in the near future. You should update your usage to make calls to the new Bucket API.'

canonical_pages.title: Bucket API endpoint (action=bucket) — structured infobox query layer
canonical_pages.data_contained: Live JSON queries over all infobox-derived structured data: items, equipment bonuses, monsters/NPCs, combat achievements, activities, etc. VERIFIED: query bucket('infobox_item').select('item_id','value').where('page_name_sub','Abyssal whip').run() returned {"bucket":[{"item_id":["4151"],"value":120001}]} (single-element item_id array, value 120001).
canonical_pages.notes: THE canonical query interface, replacing hard-deprecated SMW (action=ask VERIFIED returns badvalue: 'Unrecognized value for parameter action: ask'). Field names are case/format-sensitive: a query using 'slash_attack' on infobox_bonuses VERIFIED errors with 'Field slash_attack not found in bucket infobox_bonuses' — the real field is slash_attack_bonus (see Bucket:Infobox_bonuses). List of all buckets at Special:AllPages namespace 9592. Requires a descriptive User-Agent (wiki blocks generic agents like python-requests/curl). CORRECTION vs proposed map: proposed claimed a multi-id array [4151,4178,20405] and a different value; live infobox_item returns a single-element id array per page version and value 120001.

canonical_pages.title: Bucket:Infobox item — item schema + data preview
canonical_pages.data_contained: Schema + live data preview for the infobox_item bucket. VERIFIED exact field names: item_name (TEXT), item_id (TEXT, repeatable), image (PAGE, repeatable), examine (TEXT), value (INTEGER), high_alchemy_value (INTEGER), tradeable (BOOLEAN), buy_limit (INTEGER), is_members_only (BOOLEAN), weight (DOUBLE), quest (TEXT), release_date/removal_date (TEXT), league_region (TEXT), version_anchor (TEXT), default_version (BOOLEAN). Indexes 16,316 items.
canonical_pages.notes: CORRECTION vs proposed map: proposed listed loose labels 'high alch' and 'members'; actual fields are high_alchemy_value and is_members_only, plus buy_limit/tradeable/weight/version_anchor. Joined to infobox_bonuses via page_name_sub for equipment stats. Module:FetchItemStats queries this. There is no single Lua data module for item stats — entity stats are Bucket-served.

canonical_pages.title: Bucket:Infobox bonuses — equipment combat-bonus schema
canonical_pages.data_contained: Schema + data preview for equipment combat bonuses. VERIFIED exact field names: stab_attack_bonus, slash_attack_bonus, crush_attack_bonus, range_attack_bonus, magic_attack_bonus, stab_defence_bonus, slash_defence_bonus, crush_defence_bonus, range_defence_bonus, magic_defence_bonus, strength_bonus, ranged_strength_bonus, prayer_bonus, magic_damage_bonus, equipment_slot, weapon_attack_speed, weapon_attack_range, combat_style. Indexes 5,678 items.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Questreq/data
canonical_pages.data_contained: Quest AND achievement-diary prerequisites: for each quest/diary a 'quests' list of prerequisite quests and a 'skills' list of {skill, level, optional-modifier} tuples. VERIFIED via action=raw: ['Animal Magnetism'] includes {'Prayer',31,'ironman'},{'Ranged',30}; ['Cold War'] includes {'Woodcutting',50,'ironman','boostable'}; diaries present e.g. ['Elite Ardougne Diary'].
canonical_pages.notes: Single most valuable progression-logic module: covers quests and diaries in one Lua table with inline account-type variance — skill tuples can carry an 'ironman' modifier and a 'boostable' modifier (modifiers can combine, e.g. 'ironman','boostable'). Fetch raw at ...?action=raw. NO Module:Diary/data (VERIFIED 404); diary reqs are folded in here. Only main vs ironman is encoded — no HCIM/UIM/GIM variants.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Experience/data
canonical_pages.data_contained: The cumulative XP-per-level table (levels 1-99). VERIFIED via action=raw: it COMPUTES the table programmatically — loop 'for i=1,98 do total = math.floor(total + i + 300*math.pow(2, i/7)); ret[i+1] = math.floor(total/4) end' — rather than hardcoding values.
canonical_pages.notes: CORRECTION vs proposed map: rated 'partial' not 'yes' — it is procedural, so a naive JSON/text parse yields NO array; you must execute the Lua or reimplement the formula. Module:200mxp/data is the sibling for the 13M->200M XP / virtual-levels extension. Same XP curve for all account types.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Skill calc and per-skill submodules (Module:Skill calc/<Skill>)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Training-method XP-rate data: per-skill submodules, each an array of activities with name, level, xp, materials {name, quantity}, steps, members, type. VERIFIED via Module:Skill calc/Cooking?action=raw: { name='Bread', level=1, xp=40, materials={{name='Bread dough',quantity=1}}, steps=1, members='No', type='Baked' } and { name='Cooked meat', level=1, xp=30, materials={{name='Raw beef',quantity=1}}, steps=1, members='No', type='Meat' }.
canonical_pages.notes: URL updated to a concrete verified submodule (Module:Skill calc/Cooking) rather than the parent Module:Skill_calc. Driver loads mw.loadData('Module:Skill calc/'..skill); enumerate sibling submodules via allpages apprefix=Skill_calc. Canonical machine-readable home for 'how to train each skill'. Pulls live prices from Module:GEPrices/data.json. Fetch each submodule via action=raw.

canonical_pages.title: Module:GEPrices/data.json (+ Module:GEIDs/data.json)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Cached GE price snapshot: JSON map of item_name -> price_gp, plus '%LAST_UPDATE%' epoch and '%LAST_UPDATE_F%' human timestamp. VERIFIED via action=raw: '%LAST_UPDATE%':1781603131, '%LAST_UPDATE_F%':'16 June 2026 09:45:31 (UTC)', 'Abyssal whip':1070430, 'Air rune':5, 'Maple logs':12. Module:GEIDs/data.json is the companion item_name -> GE item id map.
canonical_pages.notes: JSON content models (fetch via action=raw). Convenient but cached/lagged on-wiki copy of prices; for fresh data prefer the Real-time Prices API below.

canonical_pages.title: RuneScape:Real-time Prices (prices.runescape.wiki API)
canonical_pages.data_contained: Docs for the live price API at prices.runescape.wiki/api/v1/osrs. VERIFIED endpoints: /latest (itemId -> {high,highTime,low,lowTime}), /mapping (id, name, examine, members(bool), lowalch, highalch, limit, icon, value), /5m, /1h, /timeseries. DMM variant at /api/v1/dmm. VERIFIED /mapping returns a JSON array, e.g. {"id":20011,"name":"3rd age axe","examine":"...","members":true,"lowalch":22000,"highalch":33000,"limit":40,"value":55000,"icon":"3rd age axe.png"}.
canonical_pages.notes: Cleanest live source for prices; /mapping is an excellent canonical item-id <-> name <-> alch-value table. VERIFIED the docs explicitly require a descriptive User-Agent and block generic agents (python-requests, curl, Java); recommended format like 'volume_tracker - @User on Discord'. Off the wiki domain but it is the wiki's official API. (Mapping covers the full tradeable item set; exact count varies — do not rely on the proposed ~3700 figure as fixed.)

canonical_pages.title: Module:Combat Achievements (logic) + combat_achievement Bucket (data)
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED: Module:Combat_Achievements is LOGIC, not a data table — it queries the Bucket: bucket('combat_achievement').select('id','name','monster','task','tier','type','league_region'). The actual task DATA is the combat_achievement Bucket. VERIFIED a live query bucket('combat_achievement').select('name','monster','tier','type').limit(3).run() returned valid JSON tasks (e.g. 'Noxious Foe','Abyssal Adept' with monster/tier/type like 'Kill Count'/'Restriction'). Completion %% in Module:Combat Achievements/completion.json.

CORRECTION: combat achievements are NOT a Lua data module — query the combat_achievement Bucket for the parseable data (fields id, name, monster, task, tier, type, league_region). Tier values: easy/medium/hard/elite/master/grandmaster.

canonical_pages.title: Module:Collection log/data.json
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED via action=raw: a JSON array of collection-log items, each {id, name, tabs:[...]} where tabs are category strings (e.g. 'Miscellaneous','Slayer','Easy Treasure Trails','All Pets'). Items can belong to multiple tabs. Example: {"id":1249,"name":"Dragon spear","tabs":["Miscellaneous"]}. Companion Module:Collection log/completion.json holds completion percentages.
canonical_pages.notes: Added in this audit (resolves a proposed-map gap). Clean, fully parseable JSON data module — the canonical machine-readable home for collection-log structure/categories.

canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED via action=raw: a Lua alias table mapping account types to Hiscores endpoints: ['main']/['default']='osrs', ['iron']/['ironman']='osrs-ironman', ['hardcore']/['hardcore ironman']='osrs-hardcore', ['ultimate']/['ultimate ironman']='osrs-ultimate', ['skiller']='osrs-skiller', ['pure']/['1-def']='osrs-pure'. (Module:Hiscores holds the rs3/osrs table identifiers.)

Note it does NOT include group ironman (GIM) or hardcore group ironman (HCGIM) — those are not encoded in the wiki machine-readable layer.

canonical_pages.title: RuneScape:Lua/Modules (module index) + MediaWiki allpages API
canonical_pages.data_contained: Index of modules in the Module: namespace (data modules, calculators, helpers). VERIFIED programmatically enumerable via api.php?action=query&list=allpages&apnamespace=828&format=json — returns JSON page titles (e.g. Module:$, Module:200mxp, Module:Abyssal pearls) with a continuation token ('apcontinue':'Abyssal_pearls/doc','continue':'-||'). Notable data modules: Experience/data, 200mxp/data, Questreq/data, GEPrices/data.json, GEIDs/data.json, Collection log/data.json, Combat Achievements, User hiscores, Hiscores.
canonical_pages.notes: Use as the discovery/enumeration entry point for the Module namespace; then fetch any module via ...?action=raw. Namespace 828 = Module, namespace 9592 = Bucket. Note enumeration includes /doc and /data.json subpages.

canonical_pages.title: Calculators (Calculator: namespace index)
canonical_pages.data_contained: VERIFIED index of interactive calculators: per-skill XP calculators (Agility, Cooking, Mining, Fishing, Prayer, ... ), Combat level, DPS Calculator, Barrows loot, Dry calculator, etc. The calculators are HTML/JavaScript front-ends (some use GEMW for live prices); underlying numbers come from the Module:Skill calc data modules.
canonical_pages.notes: Calculators themselves are interactive UI (not directly DB-parseable) — for a database use the backing data modules (Module:Skill calc/<Skill>) rather than scraping calculator pages. No dedicated quest/diary calculators; quest/diary logic lives in Module:Questreq/data.

gaps: Account-type granularity is shallow: the wiki's machine-readable requirement data only distinguishes main vs ironman (Questreq/data 'ironman' modifier). Module:User hiscores adds account-type aliases (ironman, hardcore ironman, ultimate ironman, skiller, pure) for Hiscores lookup, but there is NO structured encoding of HCIM/UIM-specific OR group-ironman (GIM/HCGIM) requirement differences — and GIM/HCGIM are absent even from the Hiscores alias map. Those nuances live in prose/notes.

Item/monster/equipment stats have NO single Lua data module — they are infobox-derived and only available via the Bucket API per-entity queries (infobox_item indexes 16,316 items, infobox_bonuses 5,678). Bulk extraction means paginating the bucket; there is no one downloadable dump.

Bucket field names are exact and unforgiving (VERIFIED: 'slash_attack' errors; the real field is 'slash_attack_bonus'). An ingestion pipeline must read each Bucket: schema page (e.g. Bucket:Infobox_item, Bucket:Infobox_bonuses) for the precise field names, which can be renamed during the ongoing SMW->Bucket migration.

SMW (action=ask) is HARD DEPRECATED and already disabled (VERIFIED returns badvalue 'Unrecognized value for parameter action: ask'). Any tooling referencing action=ask is broken now, not just at-risk; must use action=bucket.

Module:Experience/data is procedural (computes the XP curve via a formula) — a naive parse yields NO values; you must execute the Lua or reimplement total=floor(total + i + 300*2^(i/7)), ret=floor(total/4).

Skill training-method data (Module:Skill calc/<Skill>) does not encode account-type restrictions (e.g. ironmen cannot buy materials); profitability/feasibility per account type must be derived externally.

GEPrices/data.json on-wiki is a cached/lagged snapshot (VERIFIED timestamp keys %LAST_UPDATE%/%LAST_UPDATE_F%); for live prices use the separate prices.runescape.wiki API (off the wiki domain, descriptive User-Agent required).

Combat Achievements (combat_achievement Bucket) and Collection Log (Module:Collection log/data.json) are now VERIFIED and added; their completion-percentage companions (Module:Combat Achievements/completion.json, Module:Collection log/completion.json) were not deep-verified for schema in this pass.

Bucket and the Real-time Prices API both require a descriptive User-Agent (VERIFIED the prices docs explicitly block python-requests/curl/Java) and have informal rate limits — an ingestion pipeline must respect these or risk blocking.
The prices.runescape.wiki /mapping item count was not exactly counted (response was large); do not rely on the proposed ~3700 figure as a fixed number — fetch and count at runtime.
verify_changes: Re-fetched all 13 listed sources via WebFetch: confirmed Questreq/Experience/Skill calc/GEPrices modules, the Bucket API, prices API, allpages enumeration, action=ask deprecation (badvalue), and Module:Diary/data 404. CORRECTED: promoted RuneScape:Bucket docs as the canonical Bucket reference; fixed infobox_item example (single-element item_id ['4151'], value 120001) and its real field names (high_alchemy_value, is_members_only, buy_limit, version_anchor; 16,316 items); added Bucket:Infobox_bonuses with verified _bonus field names (5,678 items); downgraded Module:Experience/data to parseable=partial (procedural). ADDED to fill gaps: combat_achievement Bucket (combat achievements are Bucket-served, not a Lua module), Module:Collection log/data.json, and Module:User hiscores (account-type alias->Hiscores map: main/ironman/hardcore ironman/ultimate ironman/skiller/pure). Confirmed remaining gap: no GIM/HCGIM and no structured HCIM/UIM requirement variance.
